* Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to both technical teams and business stakeholders


**It would be ideal if you also:**
* Bring knowledge of agent testing methodologies, performance monitoring and maintaining agent reliability in production environments
* Have experience leading technical projects and mentoring team members
* Have an understanding of AI agent alignment, safety measures and ethical AI deployment in enterprise environments
* Have professional experience working in regulated industries, i.e. Banking, Government, etc.
* Have knowledge and familiarity with EU AI Act and other relevant AI regulations


 We encourage applications from candidates who are passionate about AI and meet most of these criteria. We value diverse perspectives and welcome applicants from all backgrounds.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 We kindly ask you to submit your application as soon as possible, but no later than 12/04/2026. Any applications or CVs sent by email, direct messages, or any other channel than our application forms, will not be accepted or considered.
   

  

 At Nordea, we know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable workplace. We deeply believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics, and traits make us better at serving our customers and communities.
   

  

 If you have any questions about our recruitment process, please reach out to our tech recruiter and main point of contact anna.dahlstrom@consult.nordea.com.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Poland:** 
 Please include permit for processing personal data in CV as following:
   

  

 In accordance with art. 6 (1) a and b. Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (General Data Protection Regulation) hereinafter ‘GDPR’. I agree to have: my personal data, education and employment history proceeded for the purposes of current and future recruitment processes in Nordea Bank Abp.
   

  

 The administrator of your personal data is: Nordea Bank Abp operating in Poland through its Branch, address: Aleja Edwarda Rydza Śmiglego 20, 93-281 Łodź. Your personal data will be processed for the recruitment processes in Nordea Bank Abp. You have a right to access your personal data, right to rectify and right to delete. Disclosing the personal data in the scope specified by the provisions of Polish Labour Code from 26 June 1974 and executive acts are mandatory. Providing personal data is necessary to conduct the recruitment processes. The request for the deletion of your personal data means resignation from further participation in recruitment processes and causes the immediate removal of your application. Detailed information concerning processing of your personal data can be found at: https://www.nordea.com/en/doc/nordea-privacy-policy-for-applicants.pdf
   

  

 We reserve the right to reply only to selected applications.

title: Arbetsledare
description:
**M3 Bygg söker dig som vill utvecklas och växa med oss!** 




  





**Om tjänsten och företaget** 




 Nu söker vi på M3 Bygg ytterligare en arbetsledare som kan förstärka vårt team! Det är en bred och varierande roll som är perfekt för dig som har hantverkarbakgrund eller eftergymnasial utbildning inom bygg och
 **minst tre års erfarenhet som arbetsledare** 
 .
 



  





 M3 Bygg grundades år 2000 och idag är vi ca 110 anställda. Hos oss välkomnas du till ett familjärt och prestigelöst arbetsklimat med en hög medarbetarnöjdhet och låg personalomsättning. Det är viktigt att trivas på jobbet, därför arrangerar vi kontinuerligt roliga aktiviteter tillsammans så som skidresa, golf, padel, pickleball, after works och har en vinstandelsstiftelse som samtliga medarbetare ingår i. Vi lägger stor vikt vid att ha ett schysst och trivsamt arbetsklimat på alla våra arbetsplatser. Vår gemensamma värdegrund i form av hur vi vill uppfattas sker via våra ledord: Delaktighet, Öppenhet, Flexibilitet och Förtroende. M3 Bygg är specifikt inriktade på ROT och våra projekt är indelade i tre olika verksamhetsinriktningar; Kulturminnesmärkta byggnader, Kommersiella lokaler och samhällsfastigheter samt Ombyggnad bostäder. Läs gärna mer om oss här på vår hemsida där du bland annat finner filmer om våra olika verksamhetsinriktningar samt en film där våra medarbetare beskriver hur det är att jobba på M3 Bygg.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter** 




 Rollen som arbetsledare på M3 Bygg innebär ett delegerat ansvar där du tar ansvar för ett antal underentreprenörer som du följer upp under projektets gång från start till mål. Det är en ledande roll där du har daglig kontakt med underentreprenörer och medarbetare på projektet och arbetar för att lösa eventuella hinder som uppstår. KMA-arbetet är en väldigt viktig del i arbetsledarrollen där du ser till att följa företagets rutiner och riktlinjer.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifterna sammanfattade:** 



* Ansvar över ett antal underentreprenörer på projektet
* Kontinuerlig kontakt och samarbete med medarbetare, underentreprenörer och andra aktörer kopplade till projektet
* Uppföljning av ekonomi och tidsplaner
* Administrativt arbete med KMA där du ser till att säkerställa kvalitet, miljö och arbetsmiljö i projektet



  





**Vi söker dig som:** 




 Vi söker dig som gillar att leda andra människor, är ansvarstagande och strukturerad. Du bidrar till att projektet drivs framåt att den överenskomna tidsplanen följs. Du har en god kommunikationsförmåga där du på ett tydligt och ödmjukt sätt leder medarbetare och underentreprenörer på projektet.
 



  





 Vi vill att du har minst tre års erfarenhet inom rollen som arbetsledare. Du har förmåga att ta dig an administrativa arbetsuppgifter och har en god vana av att arbeta i IT-system.
 



  





**Praktisk information** 




**Start:** 
 Omgående med hänsyn till eventuell uppsägningstid
 



**Omfattning:** 
 Heltid
 



**Anställningsform:** 
 Provanställning, max 6 månader
 



**Lön:** 
 Fast månadslön enligt överenskommelse
 



**Placering:** 
 Våra projekt är i huvudsak belägna i Storstockholm
 



**Ansökan skickas till:** 
 info@m3bygg.se

title: Director of Financial Control
description:
**Mission**
 Neko Health exists to shift healthcare from treating illness to preventing it, using advanced, non‑invasive technology and clinical expertise to deliver early, actionable health insights.
   

  

 Role Purpose
   

  

 As Director of Financial Control, you will play a central role in building and scaling Neko Health’s global financial control environment during a period of rapid international expansion.
   

  

 Working closely with the CFO and senior finance leadership, you will ensure the company maintains a robust, scalable accounting and control framework capable of supporting a complex international structure and future public-market readiness. Your work will strengthen the financial backbone of the company, enabling accurate reporting, strong governance, and financial transparency as Neko continues to grow globally.
   

  

 This role combines deep technical accounting expertise with the opportunity to build processes, systems, and teams that support a fast-scaling healthcare and technology organization.
   

  

**What You’ll Deliver in the First 6–12 Months**
* Establish a robust and scalable Group financial control framework, ensuring accurate and timely monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting across a growing international entity structure.
* Strengthen and document internal controls and governance, laying the foundation for SOX-ready compliance and supporting potential future public market transactions.
* Standardize global accounting policies and reporting practices, enabling consistent financial reporting across multiple jurisdictions and currencies.
* Improve the global month-end close process, reducing timelines while maintaining high standards of financial accuracy and audit readiness.
* Partner with ERP and finance transformation initiatives to improve financial systems, automation, and reporting infrastructure.
* Build and develop a high-performing global finance control team, establishing strong processes and clear accountability across regional controllers and reporting functions.


**Minimum Qualifications**
* 12–18+ years of progressive experience in accounting and financial control within international organizations.
* Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CPA or equivalent).
* Extensive experience managing group consolidation and financial reporting across multiple legal entities and jurisdictions.
* Strong knowledge of IFRS and complex technical accounting topics.
* Demonstrated experience building or scaling finance functions in high-growth environments.
* Experience establishing internal control frameworks and audit-ready financial processes.
* Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to communicate complex accounting matters clearly to senior leadership and non-finance stakeholders.


**Preferred**
* Experience preparing organizations for SOX compliance, IPO readiness, or public company environments.
* Background in healthcare, medical technology, SaaS, AI, or other regulated industries.
* Big 4 training or equivalent professional services background.
* Experience with ERP implementations or finance system transformations (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics).
* Proven ability to build and lead international finance teams.


**About Titles At Neko**
 We use a simplified internal title framework that prioritises clarity over hierarchy, so internal titles may differ from market‑facing role titles. Scope, impact and level of the role are fully aligned and will be clearly discussed throughout the process.
   

  

**Hiring Process**
 Candidates progress from application and structured screening through thoughtfully designed interviews culminating in a formal offer and final pre-employment checks before joining the team.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Statement**
 Neko Health is committed to inclusive hiring and member-first care. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage you to request reasonable adjustments to support your application.

title: Agil Mjukvaruutvecklare - KSU
emails: anton.eksell@ksu.se, sofia.berggren@vattenfall.com, mikael.stenmark@ksu.se, Jorgen.von.ubisch@ksu.se, ingemar.eriksson@vattenfall.com, akademikerna.vps@vattenfall.com
description:
**Vilka är vi på KSU?** 
 Vår viktigaste uppgift är att utbilda svenska kärnkraftverkens drift- och underhållspersonal.
   

  

 Idag bidrar vi till säker och stabil drift av kärnkraft och tillsammans med våra kunder skapar vi framtidens utbildning. Våra utbildningar genomförs dels i vår fullskaliga simulator samt i moderna, flexibla lärmiljöer då vi har en passion och en nyfikenhet för ny teknik.
   

  

 Nu söker vi en
 **mjukvaruutvecklare** 
 som vill vara med och utveckla nästa generations digitala lösningar inom en växande och innovativ verksamhet.
   

  

**Om jobbet**
 Som mjukvaruutvecklare blir du en del av ett mindre, dynamiskt team där samarbete och innovation står i fokus. Du arbetar med att utveckla, testa och implementera robusta och skalbara lösningar som möter både interna och externa kunders behov.
   

  

 Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter som mjukvaruutvecklare i en agil miljö:
   

  

* Utveckla och vidareutveckla mjukvarulösningar
* Testa, implementera och underhålla system och produkter
* Arbeta med databaser, nätverkskommunikation och systemintegration
* Bidra till kontinuerliga förbättringar av befintliga lösningar
* Skapa dokumentation, manualer och stötta användare


 Möjliga placeringsorter är där KSU har utbildningssiter, i närheten av de tre kärnkraftverken vid Forsmark, Ringhals och Oskarshamn, huvudkontoret för KSU är i Nyköping annan placeringsort kan diskuteras.
   

  

 Kvalifikationer
   

  

 Vi söker dig som är analytisk, nyfiken och trivs i en miljö där inte alla svar är givna från början.
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som**
* Har erfarenhet av att ha arbetat som mjukvaruutvecklare ett par år
* Har kunskap i exempelvis JavaScript, C# eller Python
* Har erfarenhet av runt implementation, konfiguration och tillämpning av AI-modeller.
* Har jobbat mycket i team och ser Git som en hygienfaktor
* Har en relevant högskole- eller civilingenjörsutbildning inom IT/data
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska


**Det Är Meriterande Om Du Har Erfarenhet Av**
* Nätverkskommunikation och säkerhet
* Autentisering och säkra systemlösningar
* 3D-grafik, spelmotorer eller CAD


**Som Person Är Du**
* Analytisk och lösningsorienterad
* Strukturerad med fokus på förbättring
* Bekväm med att arbeta i osäkerhet och driva arbetet framåt


**Om oss**
 Du blir en del av en verksamhet i stark tillväxt där vi utvecklar framtidens digitala träningsmiljöer, både nationellt och internationellt. Här får du arbeta nära kollegor med olika kompetenser i ett öppet och samarbetsinriktat klimat.
   

  

 Ytterligare information
   

  

**Vi erbjuder**
 Förutom att du får vara med och forma framtidens smarta energisamhälle erbjuder vi dig ett utvecklande och omväxlande arbete med många kontaktytor. För oss på Vattenfall är det viktigt att arbete och privatliv har en god balans, därför erbjuder vi flexibilitet i arbetet samt möjlighet till att arbeta hemifrån vid behov. Vi har även en hel del andra personalförmåner som exempelvis arbetstidsförkortning, förmånliga tjänstepensionsavtal, föräldraledighetstillägg med mera. Läs mer om våra förmåner här.
   

  

**Placeringsort** 
 är Ringhals, Oskarshamn, Forsmark, Nyköping eller Solna
   

  

**För ytterligare information** 
 om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta rekryterande chef Anton Eksell, anton.eksell@ksu.se. För frågor om rekryteringsprocessen, vänligen kontakta rekryterare Sofia Berggren, sofia.berggren@vattenfall.com.
   

  

**Fackliga representanter är** 
 Mikael Stenmark, mikael.stenmark@ksu.se (Unionen), Jörgen von Ubisch, Jorgen.von.ubisch@ksu.se (Ledarna), Ingemar Eriksson, ingemar.eriksson@vattenfall.com (SEKO) och Gustaf Treiber, akademikerna.vps@vattenfall.com (Akademikerna).
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 19 april. Vi ser fram emot just din ansökan så tveka inte - sök redan i dag!**
 Vi tar inte emot personligt brev i ansökan. Du söker snabbt och enkelt genom att svara på urvalsfrågor och bifoga ditt CV. I den här rekryteringsprocessen kommer tester att förekomma.
   

  

 På Vattenfall värdesätter vi att vara aktiva, positiva, öppna och säkerhetsmedvetna. Vi söker medarbetare som delar vår vision och kan bidra till att stärka vår företagskultur. Vi är övertygade om att mångfald bidrar till att bygga ett mer lönsamt och tilltalande företag och strävar efter att vara goda förebilder när det gäller mångfald. Vattenfall arbetar aktivt för att alla medarbetare ska ha samma möjligheter och rättigheter oavsett ålder, etnisk eller kulturell bakgrund, könstillhörighet, religion/tro, sexuell läggning eller funktionsvariation. Läs mer om hur vi arbetar med mångfald och inkludering här.
   

  

*Vattenfall är en del av Sveriges kritiska infrastruktur och vår verksamhet omfattas bland annat av regler om säkerhetsskydd, nukleär icke-spridning och exportkontroll. På grund av dessa regler genomförs en kontroll av kandidaten för många av tjänsterna i vår verksamhet innan anställning sker. För de tjänster som leder till deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet eller placering i säkerhetsklass innebär denna kontroll att säkerhetsprövning genomförs i enlighet med säkerhetsskyddslagen. Förutom säkerhetsprövning genomförs även drogtest. Du kan komma att bli krigsplacerad. Eventuell krigsplacering sker med stöd i anställningsavtalet och lagen om totalförsvarsplikt.*
**Vi ser fram mot din ansökan!**
 Företagsbeskrivning
   

  

***KSU, Kärnkraftsäkerhet och Utbildning AB** 
 , kompetenssäkrar de svenska kärnkraftverkens personal genom såväl teoretisk utbildning som praktisk träning i fullskalesimulatorer. Företaget befinner sig i utvecklingsfas och vi ser en spännande framtid för att säkerställa kompetens för långsiktig och ny kärnkraftsdrift.*
*Verksamheten bedrivs från vårt huvudkontor som ligger i Nyköping samt från våra träningsanläggningar som ligger i nära anslutning till kärnkraftverken i Forsmark, Oskarshamn och Ringhals. KSU, med ca 180 anställda, delägs av Forsmarks Kraftgrupp AB, OKG AB, Ringhals AB och ingår i Vattenfallkoncernen, Business Unit Vattenfall Power Solutions, VPS.*
*Du kliver in i en spännande tid och får möjligheten att var en viktig kugge att skapa KSUs framtid för att bidra till fossilfri framtid och kompetens för framtidens kärnkraft.*

title: AI Model Governance Analyst (Financial Crime Prevention)
description:
**Job ID: 3522**
 We are looking for an AI Model Governance Analyst to join our team as a full time employee.
   

  

**About Our Team**
 Meet the AI & Machine Learning team. Our role is to improve how we detect and disrupt financial crime with the help of AI and machine learning, to keep our customers, Nordea and society safe. Our team is about 25 people working out of Stockholm, Helsinki and Copenhagen as a part of the group financial crime prevention (GFCP) unit that provides financial crime prevention services to all of the bank.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**Main responsibilities in this role:**
* Regulatory compliance: ensuring adherence to global regulations (e.g., EU AI Act, GDPR, CPRA) and industry-specific standards.
* Risk Management & Mitigation: working cooperatively with the teams to identify, assess, and mitigate risks such as algorithmic bias, data privacy, and security vulnerabilities.
* Model Monitoring & Audit: developing processes for monitoring model performance, explainability, observability and maintaining documentation/audit trails for all AI models.
* Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partnering with legal, compliance, model validation, data science, IT, and business units to ensure ongoing compliance.


**Who You Are**
 This is the right role if you have a passion to make both AI and the world a better place.
   

  

**Your background and skills include:**
* Domain Expertise: 5+ years in financial crime risk management, legal/compliance, data governance, or technology auditing.
* AI Knowledge: 3+ years in machine learning (ML) lifecycles, model failure modes, and generative AI risks.
* Communication: Excellent ability to bridge technical, business, and legal teams to explain AI risks and benefits.


 It would be ideal if you also have completed a specialized certificate in AI and compliance, such as ICA Specialist Certificate in AI for Compliance Specialists or IAPP's Artificial Intelligence Governance Professional (AIGP).
   

  

**What We Offer**
**People** 
 come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

**A culture** 
 that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

**Hybrid working model** 
 – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion** 
 are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 Submit your application as soon as possible as we start with interviews on ongoing basis. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Björn Hertzberg at bjorn.hertzberg@nordea.com.
   

  

**We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.**
**We build relationships.** 
 We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.
   

  

**We learn and develop.** 
 We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.
   

  

**We lead change.** 
 We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.
   

  

**We are Nordea.** 
 We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Finland:** 
 A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com.

title: Account Manager
description:
**The Next Level of Fire & Life Safety Leadership**
 With more than two centuries of combined history, brands like Kidde, Kidde Commercial, Edwards, GST, Badger, Gloria and Aritech have been leading the way in protecting people and property around the world. Now, we’re taking that leadership to the next level. Kidde Global Solutions brings together the most trusted and iconic brands in safety, building upon our shared experience and expertise to address the needs and challenges of more customers in more places. With an unmatched portfolio of solutions and services, and a commitment to constant innovation we’re elevating the standards for an entire industry – and establishing the next level of fire and life safety leadership.
   

  

 At Kidde Global Solutions, you’re joining a global technology leader in fire and life safety solutions and our employees are the heart of it all. With industry-defining brands in life safety and security, you’ll be part of a legacy of leadership working with cutting-edge technologies that protect lives and property worldwide. As a leading fire safety brand, our mission is to provide solutions that protect people and property from the effects of fire and related hazards. We’re committed to expanding our impact and providing dynamic opportunities for growth within our team. You’ll be part of a company that prioritizes quality, integrity, collaboration, and customer care, with the resources, training, and incentives to ensure your success every step of the way.
   

  

**Account Manager Sweden**
**Location:** 
 On-site in Stockholm/Kista
   

  

**Job Type:** 
 Full-time
   

  

**Aritech**
**A brand with a legacy — and a future**
 Aritech has been a trusted name in the security industry for decades. Our product portfolio is recognized for reliability, innovation, and performance. You will represent a brand that customers already trust — and help shape the next chapter as we continue to grow in Sweden.
   

  

 You won’t just “manage sales” — you will contribute to building the Swedish market.
   

  

 We give you the freedom, ownership, and decision-making power to create long-term impact. Your ideas will be heard. Your strategy will matter. Your success will be visible.
   

  

 Although you will work independently in the field, you’ll be strongly supported by our Nordic sales organization, customer service, technical support, and marketing. At Aritech, we win together — across borders, functions, and expertise.
   

  

**Your Role And Responsibilities**
* Sales Strategy & Market Growth
* Build and execute the Swedish sales strategy with full ownership
* Increase revenue, market share, and partner engagement
* Identify and develop new business opportunities
* Become the go-to commercial contact for partners and customers nationwide
* Build long-lasting partnerships with installers, integrators, and distributors
* Conduct frequent customer visits (approx. 25 travel days/year)
* Strengthen customer loyalty through trust, service, and expertise
* Represent Aritech at industry events, fairs, and seminars
* Product & Solution Expertise
* Become an expert in Aritech’s advanced security technologies
* Communicate complex technical solutions in a clear, value-driven way
* Bring market trends and customer insights into the Nordic sales team
* Soft Skills & Competencies
* Problem-solving mindset
* Ability to work cross-functionally
* Adaptability in a growing market
* Reporting & Performance Management
* Manage your pipeline and forecasts in Salesforce
* Analyze performance, KPIs, and conversion rates
* Act proactively when course correction is needed


**What You Bring**
* several years of B2B sales experience
* Ideally a background in security, technology, electronics, or similar
* Excellent communication, relationship-building, and negotiation skills
* Fluency in Swedish and English
* Strong ownership mindset, independence, and commercial drive
* Curiosity for technology and willingness to learn


**What We Offer**
* Competitive base salary aligned with the Swedish market
* SIP bonus of up to 25%
* Company car, laptop, phone, and tools for your success
* A respected global brand with a high-quality product range
* The opportunity to shape Aritech’s success in Sweden
* A collaborative Nordic team that supports you every step of the way


 The final compensation for this position will be set based on the individual’s knowledge, skills, and experience as it relates to the job requirements.
   

  

**Your journey with us**
 We Always Aim To Provide a Fair And Valuable Recruitment Process For Everyone. If Selected, You Will Follow The Next Stages
   

  

* Initial call with our recruitment team
* Interviews with the Hiring Manager and key stakeholders (up to 3 interviews)


**Our Commitment to You**
 As an employer of choice, our greatest assets are the expertise, creativity, and passion of our employees. We strive to create a workplace that attracts, develops, and retains top talent, promotes engagement, fosters teamwork, and drives innovation for the benefit of our customers. We aim to build an environment where you feel a true sense of belonging, with diversity and inclusion as key drivers of growth and innovation.
   

  

 We provide best-in-class programs and practices, offer enriching career opportunities, actively listen to employee feedback, and continually challenge ourselves to do better. The values that guide us are
 **respect, integrity, inclusion, innovation, and excellence** 
 .
   

  

 We’re excited for you to join us and make a real difference.
   

  

**World Leaders in Fire and Life Safety**
 Kidde Global Solutions is a world leader in fire & life safety solutions tailored for complex commercial facilities to homes. Through iconic, industry-defining brands including Kidde, Kidde Commercial, Edwards, GST, Badger, Gloria and Aritech, we provide residential and commercial customers with advanced solutions and services to protect people and property in a wide range of applications, all around the globe.

title: Content Planning Manager
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for a senior, strategically minded
 **Content Planning Manager** 
 to shape and optimize our acquired content portfolio across the Nordics.
   

  

 The role
   

  

 As Content Planning Manager, you will own the long-term strategic planning of our acquired portfolio across SVOD and PTV. Your mission is to translate strategy and budgets into a clear, commercially optimized content roadmap that maximizes impact across territories and platforms.
   

  

 You will act as the central planning authority - connecting acquisitions, local programming teams, performance insights and editorial priorities - while leading and developing the Content Planning team.
   

  

 This is a highly cross-functional role at the heart of our content organization.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Build and own the annual and quarterly master content plan, setting priorities, tentpoles and focus periods in line with ROI targets and lifecycle performance.
* Optimize portfolio pacing and budget allocation to maximize commercial impact across platforms and territories.
* Drive alignment across Movies, Series, Factual and Kids, balancing central strategy with local market needs.
* Partner with local teams to ensure territorial plans are commercially sound and delivered with precision.
* Prevent internal cannibalization across content models through clear prioritization.
* Translate content strategy into acquisition timing and deal requirements.
* Lead scenario planning and proactively identify risks, overlaps and gaps.
* Proactively identify creative angles within the content slate on how titles can be amplified across the business, including launch concepts, thematic programming, and alternative exploitation opportunities.
* Lead and develop the Content Planning team with clear direction, accountability and a performance-focused culture.


 What we’re looking for
   

  

 You are a structured and forward-thinking senior planner with strong commercial instincts. You understand that planning is not only about structure - it’s about timing, positioning, prioritization, and ultimately driving business performance and growth.
   

  

 You have experience within content strategy, portfolio planning, acquisitions or media planning - ideally gained in a streaming, broadcast or media environment. You have a solid understanding of how content is scheduled, windowed and monetized across platforms, Experience leading or coordinating teams is a plus.
   

  

**You Are**
* Experienced in analyzing performance data, audience insights and/or viewing trends.
* Proven ability to translate commercial goals and performance targets into actionable content plans.
* Strong in cross-functional and cross-territory collaboration.
* Experienced in working with budgets, ROI thinking and targets.
* Comfortable balancing central strategy with local market needs.
* Proactive in identifying risks and opportunities early.
* Knowledgeable about the streaming and media landscape.
* Fluent in Sweden and English.
* Passionate about movies and series.


 What we offer you
   

  

**We’ve got you covered!** 
 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
   

  

**Your wellbeing matters.** 
 We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
   

  

**A safe space to grow and up-skill.** 
 Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
   

  

**Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do** 
 . So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including series & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.
   

  

 Hit play today
   

  

 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: People Manager - Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE
description:
På Capgemini får du chansen att göra verklig skillnad – för människor, företag och samhället. Hos oss får du #MakeItReal på riktigt, med stöd av kollegor som inspirerar, stöttar och utmanar dig att växa varje dag. Vi brinner för digital innovation och söker dig som vill skapa nästa generations smarta lösningar tillsammans med oss. Inom vårt område Digital Customer Experience (DCX) utvecklar vi lösningar som förbättrar hur människor interagerar med digitala tjänster, med hjälp av smart design, data och teknik. Tillsammans med världsledande plattformar bygger vi innovationer som gör skillnad för människor, företag och en mer hållbar framtid. Hos oss får du möjlighet att arbeta med det senaste inom Microsofts ekosystem – inklusive AI-drivna lösningar som Copilot i Dynamics 365 CE och Power Platform.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som People Manager för Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE i Sverige leder du ett team av erfarna konsulter och ansvarar för deras kompetensutveckling och framdrift. Du leder utvecklingen av vårt Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE område i Sverige och driver affären framåt. Du sätter den strategiska riktningen för teamet, utvecklar affärsområdet och bidrar i försäljning och leverans som senior konsult eller rådgivare. Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med kundansvariga/säljare, Nordiskt CoE och du ingår i en chefsgrupp där ni tillsammans ansvarar för operativ styrning och affärsplanering.
 



  





*I denna roll kommer du att få:* 




  




* Formellt personalansvar inklusive lönesättning och kompetensutveckling.
* Ansvar för beläggning och lönsamhet för teamet.
* Ansvar för att teamets konsulter har rätt kompetens i förhållande till våra kunders behov och vår framtida strategiska riktning.
* Ansvar för att driva tillväxt och rekrytera nya förmågor till teamet.
* Delta i leverans och försäljning som senior konsult eller rådgivare.



  





**Din profil** 



* Gedigen förståelse för verksamhetsutveckling och teknologi samt erfarenhet av att leda utvecklingsprojekt hos kund.
* Flera års erfarenhet av projekt och leveranser inom teknikområdet, gärna som konsult.
* God insikt i hur Dynamics 365 CE, tillsammans med Power Platform, kan skapa affärsnytta i kunders sälj- och serviceprocesser.
* Förmåga att bygga organisationer och leveransteam samt att förklara tekniska lösningar på ett sätt som skapar förståelse hos kunder och internt.
* Erfarenhet av att leda och coacha team. Har du haft personalansvar är det ett plus.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska och relevant högskoleexamen eller motsvarande erfarenhet.



  





**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas** 



* Ett härligt team med högt i tak
* Komptensutveckling i form av utbildningar och certifieringar.
* Flexibla arbetsförhållanden & Work-from-Home Policy.
* Generöst friskvårdsbidrag, sjukvårdsförsäkring & tjänstepension.



  





**Ansökan** 




 Redo att starta ett nytt kapitel i karriären? Vi kan knappt vänta på att få höra från dig! Ansök redan idag – vi hanterar ansökningarna löpande. Under processen får du chansen att kika in på vårt kontor, prata med vår rekryterande chef och självklart ställa alla dina nyfikna frågor till vår rekryterare. Vi lovar att göra din resa mot jobbet så smidig och givande som möjligt.
 



  





*Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov.* 




  





**Om Capgemini** 




 Capgemini är en AI-driven global partner för affärs- och tekniktransformation som levererar konkret verksamhetsnytta. Vi formar framtiden för organisationer och förverkligar den med hjälp av AI, teknik och människor. Med vår starka historia på nästan 60 år är vi en ansvarsfull och mångsidig koncern med 420 000 medarbetare i över 50 länder. Genom vår expertis inom strategi, teknik, design, ingenjörstjänster och affärsverksamhet levererar vi heltäckande tjänster och lösningar, baserade på vår djupa branschkunskap och vårt omfattande partnernätverk. Koncernen rapporterade globala intäkter på 22,1 miljarder euro för 2024.
 



**Make it real** 
 | www.capgemini.com

title: Sr. Product Advisor - Learning
description:
**Your work days are brighter here.**
 We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.
   

  

**About The Team**
 Product Acceleration and Optimization (PAO) is an organization within our Product team. Our mission is to enable customers and our services ecosystem to adopt Workday products and solutions in the most effective and efficient manner and with the highest quality to ensure lasting customer success.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for someone who is passionate about partnering with our Product, Sales and Professional Services teams to ensure the successful implementation of Talent Management with a focus on Learning and sub-expertise in Talent Optimization and Peakon Employee Voice.
   

  

 The Sr. Product Advisor drives enablement through the following programs:
   

  

* Engaging with Early Adopter Customers and Strategic Customers in product escalations and customer corporate visits (as needed).
* Supporting New Offering Launches and Acquisitions, enabling consultants, managing Product Leads aligned to your product area.
* Advising Product Managers and Development on customer use cases, deployment considerations to ensure the launch of solid minimum viable products.
* Engaging in Innovative Sales Pursuits


 As a Sr. Product Advisor, you will:
   

  

* Drive deployment efficiency by influencing the product roadmap as an advocate of Customer and Services feedback
* Advise on and influence Product and Development teams on the product design (oCHRO business challenges, use cases and requirements for a solid minimum viable product) and deployability, advocating for Services and Customer feedback
* Strategize how to leverage agentic technology to reduce deployment cost and effort
* Work with strategic customers deploying new products to ensure successful deployments
* Develop early deployment guidance on New Offering Launches and Acquisitions
* Collaborate with the broader Workday ecosystem to identify, architect and build technical solutions to reduce deployment efforts and escalations
* Support strategic sales opportunities involving new products, features, or complex solutions


**About You**
 Basic Qualifications:
   

  

* 3-5 years experience with domain expertise in enterprise software applications, specifically Human Capital Management with a solid background in Learning Management solutions.
* 2-3+ years deploying or supporting Learning Management solutions


 Other Qualifications:
   

  

* Is adaptable and can adjust to changing circumstances and priorities
* Communicates and collaborates effectively.
* Learns new technologies and related product areas quickly.
* Can handle multiple competing priorities.
* Has strong problem-solving abilities.
* Explains technical concepts to those with less technical knowledge.
* Mentors consultants and leads small teams.
* Willing to travel up to 25% of the time.


**Workday Pay Transparency Statement**
 The annualized base salary ranges for the primary location and any additional locations are listed below. Workday pay ranges vary based on work location. As a part of the total compensation package, this role may be eligible for the Workday Bonus Plan or a role-specific commission/bonus, as well as annual refresh stock grants. Recruiters can share more detail during the hiring process. Each candidate’s compensation offer will be based on multiple factors including, but not limited to, geography, experience, skills, job duties, and business need, among other things. For more information regarding Workday’s comprehensive benefits in Canada, please click here. For more information regarding Workday’s comprehensive benefits in the US, please click here.
   

  

 Primary Location: IRL.Dublin.Kings
   

  

 Additional US Location(s) Base Pay Range: $125,400 USD - $222,700 USD
   

  

 Additional CAN Location(s) Base Pay Range: $122,100 - $183,100 CAD
   

  

**Our Approach to Flexible Work**
 With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply
 **spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field** 
 with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
   

  

 Pursuant to applicable Fair Chance law, Workday will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.
   

  

 Workday is an Equal Opportunity Employer including individuals with disabilities and protected veterans.
   

  

 At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.
   

  

**Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!**
 At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security. Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers.
   

  

 Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.
   

  

 In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.
   

  

 ,

title: HR Generalist-1 year contract
description:
**HR Generalist**
**Do you want to contribute in our mission to make people and businesses shine in any language? Do you thrive best in an eventful environment and in an international company that is undergoing a journey of change and growth where HR has a central role? And are you motivated by working with managerial support linked to HR? Then Semantix looks forward to welcoming you to its HR team!**
 Semantix offers you a broad HR Generalist role where you are an important and natural part of both strategic discussions and the operational HR work with the aim of contributing to business development. In this role you would provide HR support to our employees in Sweden and also support when needed to Nordic region and managers in different cities and countries.
   

  

**Your overall responsibilities:**
 Support leaders with HR related matters.
   

  

**Be responsible for the payroll process and systems, contact person to outsourced payroll.**
 Be responsible for the systematic work environment cycle and the Swedish health and safety committee.
   

  

 Union collaboration according to Semantix collective bargaining agreement (Tjänstemannaavtalet).
   

  

 Perform risk assessments and union negotiations with local representatives.
   

  

 Manage projects within HR, both on local and group level, e.g. our Nordic HR integration project.
   

  

**Work with HR Manager on e.g. salary review and equal pay survey.**
 Support culture development and continuously implement the Semantix way.
   

  

 Recruitment e.g. publish ads, conduct interviews, collaborate with recruitment partners, write contracts etc.
   

  

 On- and offboarding.
   

  

 Maintain and develop guidelines, instructions and processes.
   

  

 Maintain HR systems and ensure the adequacy of HR data.
   

  

**HR Reporting And Other HR Administrative Tasks.**
 You report to Semantix HR Manager in Stockholm, Sweden, and are part of Semantix HR team with colleagues in the Nordics and Croatia. You also belong to TransPerfect’s large, international HR team. The culture is characterized by a welcoming and open work climate, both in relation to coming up with new ideas, efficiency and improvement proposals, but also in relation to the atmosphere in the office. We’re encouraging our people to be self-starting, helpful, including, naturally curios and entrepreneurial. Part of our leadership philosophy is that it’s less about being the best and more about helping others be their best.
   

  

**As a person** 
 , we see that you are confident and independent in your role as HR Generalist. You are thorough and structured in your work and used to international collaboration. You have experience of most parts in the HR area. We believe that you are driven, unpretentious and flexible. You are good at driving processes forward and are not afraid to come up with ideas and to influence managers and employees. Influencing and developing processes comes naturally to you and you do it in an independent and professional way.
   

  

**We believe you have:**
**About 3-5 Years’ Experience In Broad HR Work.**
 An academic education in HR or equivalent.
   

  

 Full working proficiency in both Swedish and English.
   

  

**Knowledge in Swedish labour law.**
 Experience from union collaboration.
   

  

 Experience from white collar workers’ collective bargaining agreements is an advantage.
   

  

 Experience from an international organization is a great advantage.
   

  

**More About The Job**
 The position is full-time and located at our office in Stockholm Östermalm.
   

  

 The position is a fixed-term maternity leave cover expected to be from June 2026 to July 2027.
   

  

**About The Company**
 Semantix is the largest language service provider in the Nordics – and aims to become the market leader in language technology and multilingual services. We live by guiding principles like “good judgement before policy” and “problem solving before problem dwelling” and the core values “Together as one, we create value and drive progress”. Read more about Semantix in our webpage. Semantix is part of the TransPerfect family of companies.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 This recruitment is a collaboration with TransPerfect’s in-house recruitment team. If you have questions about the role or if you’d like to apply please contact hiring manager Susanne Björnhöfde, Susanne.bjornhofde@semantix.com
   

  

 The selection will be done continuously, please submit your application no later than April xx 2026.

title: Senior HR Business Advisor
description:
At Tele2, we challenge the telecom industry to create more valuable solutions for our customers. Guided by our challenger mindset, we deliver high-quality connectivity and digital services, ranging from mobile and fixed networks to TV, streaming and global IoT.
   

  

 Sustainability is an integral part of how we work. Tele2 has been recognized as a climate leader in Europe by the Financial Times and named Sweden’s most sustainable company, reflecting our long-term commitment to reducing our environmental impact and advancing circular solutions together with our customers.
   

  

 Our values – Be Brave, Take Action, Make it Simple and Act Cost-efficiently – guide our culture, how we collaborate and how we turn ideas into action.
   

  

*We are looking for an energetic, flexible, and driven senior HR professional with years of experience. Deep knowledge of the broad HR portfolio and how to connect it to the business is your key asset. You have broad experience in leading business transformations, workforce planning and putting strategy into practice.*
*You are expected to jointly drive and engage others around our people strategy, organization development and people review. You have experience of working closely with executives and are comfortable as an escalation point for a wide range of employee matters. If you enjoy the mixture of strategic thinking, tactics, and operations, you will thrive in this role.*
**Key responsibilities:**
* Provide support and oversee organization development, transformation, and change management activities
* Conduct proactive workforce planning, including people review, skill assessments, performance management, forecasting, and succession management
* Steer Business Units union negotiations council and work environment committee for Business Units specific needs
* Drive the overall people plan for a key business unit
* Advise and coach the unit’s leadership teams on people’s matters
* Handle escalations in employee conflicts and disciplinary actions in accordance with company policies
* Act as a strong role model, delivering solutions and results that meet business needs
* Oversee and facilitate the development and implementation of culture, leadership, engagement, and D&I activities across the business unit


**Who are you?**
 Tele2 is well known for its high pace, fast decision-making, and ability to quickly adapt to change. In this role, you constantly seek improvement, deal well with ambiguity, and work independently. We are looking for a colleague with a proactive approach, an end-to-end operation and customer first mindset. You seek creative solutions, deliver results, and most importantly, you embrace Tele2’s culture.
   

  

**Qualifications:**
* University degree or similar education within Human Resources, Economics, or Business Management
* Several years of HR portfolio experience; you have either deep experience as a senior HR Business Advisor or are currently working in a senior HR manager position
* Education and/or practical experience with Swedish labor law
* Experience in organization development and change management
* Experience in project management, including proven ability to lead others to achieve a common goal or delivery
* Excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English
* Business experience in the Tech and/or Telco area


**Benefits**
 Pension and health care
   

  

 Hybrid and flexible work life balance
   

  

 Career opportunities
   

  

 Parental benefit
   

  

 Employee benefits and Tele2 discounts
   

  

 Inclusive workplace

title: Internship: Consumption-based accounting, modelling and analysis
description:
**Introduction**
 Join the Cities, Communities and Consumption team at SEI HQ as an intern and contribute to cutting-edge research on consumption-based emissions. You will support the development of the Consumption Compass tool and help generate insights to inform policies for a sustainable and just transition.
   

  

**Project/internship introduction**
 The project Decoding climate footprints – User-centric solutions for bridging climate footprints and climate justice at the local level is seeking an intern to support the next phase of the Consumption Compass (Konsumtionskompassen) .
   

  

 The Consumption Compass is an innovative tool that downscales Sweden’s consumption-based emissions from the national level to postcode level across more than 100 consumption categories. It is widely used by Swedish municipalities and other stakeholders to better understand local consumption patterns and design policies that support more sustainable and equitable lifestyles.
   

  

 The project is now focusing on improving the model and expanding the tool’s functionality. This includes analysing how household carbon footprints relate to socio-demographic variables such as income, education, household composition and housing type. It also includes developing a library of policy measures to support municipalities in reducing consumption-based emissions.
   

  

 As an intern, you will contribute to both quantitative and qualitative work, including data collection, modelling support and statistical analysis, as well as engagement with project partners.
   

  

**Tasks**
* conduct quantitative and qualitative data analysis, including analysing consumption and footprint data and visualizing their results
* supporting model development and validation, including data processing and exploratory analysis (e.g. in Excel, Python)
* testing new versions of the tool and provide structured feedback


 Additional tasks may include:
   

  

* supporting the development of a policy library on measures to reduce consumption-based emissions
* assisting with workshops with project partners, including preparing materials, summarizing discussions and supporting follow-up


 This internship may also be carried out as part of a thesis project.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You are a student with an interest in sustainable consumption and climate policy. You are studying environmental science, sustainability science, data science, economics or a related field, with a focus on environmental policy, statistics or quantitative social science methods.
   

  

 You have experience working with quantitative data and are comfortable using Excel. Experience with statistical analysis and programming (e.g. Python) is an advantage. Familiarity with carbon footprinting or consumption-based accounting is also an asset.
   

  

 You are collaborative, proactive and able to work independently, with strong organizational skills. As the work focuses on a Swedish context, proficiency in Swedish is highly desirable, particularly for engaging with stakeholders and materials.
   

  

**Skills And Experience**
* currently enrolled in a master’s program
* fluent in English; proficiency in Swedish is highly desirable
* proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
* experience with quantitative data analysis
* programming skills (e.g. Python) are an asset
* strong analytical, organizational and time management skills


**Location**
 The internship is based at SEI HQ in Stockholm. The intern is expected to be in the office 1–2 days per week.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We review applications on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible, and no later than 19 April 2026, 23:59 (Stockholm time).
   

  

 Applications should be written in English and include a short CV and cover letter (maximum one page).
   

  

 Please include in your cover letter:
   

  

* what you would like to do and learn during your internship and your strengths related to the role
* how the internship relates to your studies and future plans


 Your CV should include one reference (name, affiliation and contact details).
   

  

**Internships at SEI**
 We aim to attract competent and skilled university students for our internships. Our goal is to support the intern’s development by providing a valuable learning experience in a professional setting. To be an intern you need to be enrolled at a university or university college. The internship should be part of your education. We only offer unpaid internships. Applicants must be fully funded and insured by external sources throughout their time at SEI HQ.
   

  

 All SEI recruitments are based on respect for the individual regardless of gender, gender identity or expression, age, union representation or political opinion, functional variation, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity, nationality and marital or parental status.
   

  

**Questions?**
* For questions about the aims of the internship, please contact Katarina Axelsson, Senior Policy Fellow axelsson@sei.org .
* For questions about the recruitment process, please contact Filippa Klintberg, HR Specialist klintberg@sei.org .


**Meet the team**
 Learn more about the Cities, Communities and Consumption team and their work, as well as the Consumption Compass tool and team , as well as related projects, on our website.
   

  

**About SEI**
 Stockholm Environment Institute is an international non-profit research institute that tackles climate, environment and sustainable development challenges.
   

  

 We empower partners to meet these challenges through cutting-edge research, knowledge, tools and capacity building. Scientific quality and integrity are foundations of our work. Partnership is at the heart of our approach, leading to change that lasts.
   

  

 Our work connects science to policy and practice, aiming to drive tangible impacts. It spans climate change, natural resources, water, air, and health, and addresses questions of governance, innovation, finance, poverty, gender equality and social equity.
   

  

 We are committed to transparency and full disclosure of our funding. The Government of Sweden is our largest funder, and we also receive funds from a range of public research funders, philanthropic foundations, bilateral and multilateral development agencies, governments, NGOs and other partners.
   

  

 Through SEI’s HQ and seven centres around the world, we engage with policy, practice and development action for a sustainable, prosperous future for all.

title: Data & AI specialist med Scrum Master-erfarenhet
description:
Eccera söker nu en Data & AI Specialist med Scrum Master-erfarenhet till ett spännande uppdrag hos vår kund inom försvarsindustrin i Stockholm.
 



  





 Vill du arbeta med banbrytande AI-lösningar och vara med och möjliggöra användningen av AI och Machine Learning i avancerade tekniska system? Då kan detta vara rätt uppdrag för dig.
 



  





 I rollen kommer du att arbeta i ett senior team med fokus på utveckling, implementering och drift av cloud-native Data & AI-plattformar. Rollen kombinerar tekniskt ledarskap inom AI- och DevOps-miljöer med ansvar som Scrum Master där du leder teamets agila arbete och säkerställer effektivt samarbete mellan utvecklingsteam och produktägare.
 



  





 Tjänsten är placerad i säkerhetsklass, vilket innebär att säkerhetsprövning kommer att genomföras.
 



  





 Som konsult på Eccera får du en trygg anställning och samlar på dig värdefull arbetslivserfarenhet på uppdrag hos våra kunder. Din konsultchef stöttar dig i din utveckling där du hela tiden fyller på med nya erfarenheter och kunskaper. En utmärkt möjlighet att göra karriär.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Data & AI Specialist kommer du att ha en central roll i utvecklingen och förvaltningen av organisationens AI-plattformar. Du arbetar nära både utvecklingsteam, plattformsteam och interna intressenter för att säkerställa att Data & AI-lösningar är säkra, skalbara och anpassade efter verksamhetens behov.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter** 




  





*I rollen kommer du bland annat att:* 



* Leda teamet enligt Scrum/SAFe och facilitera sprintplanering, daily standups, refinements och retrospectives
* Arbeta hands-on med utveckling, installation, konfiguration och drift av cloud-native Data & AI-plattformar
* Samarbeta med interna intressenter för att integrera AI-verktyg och plattformar i produkter och tjänster
* Arbeta med CI/CD-pipelines och automatiserade leveransflöden
* Ge tekniskt ledarskap och mentorskap till teammedlemmar
* Felsöka och hantera tekniska problem i AI- och DevOps-miljöer
* Säkerställa att AI-miljöer är stabila, säkra och optimerade för prestanda
* Dokumentera processer och ta fram riktlinjer för AI-plattformar



  





 Resor kan förekomma i tjänsten.
 



  





**Din profil** 




  





*Vi söker dig som har:* 



* Kandidatexamen eller master inom datavetenskap, elektronik eller annat relevant tekniskt område, alternativt motsvarande erfarenhet
* Erfarenhet av mjukvaruutveckling
* Erfarenhet av att installera, konfigurera och drifta AI- eller DevOps-miljöer
* God förståelse för AI-koncept och praktisk tillämpning av AI/ML
* Erfarenhet av Scrum Master-rollen, gärna inom SAFe
* Erfarenhet av agila arbetssätt och verktyg som Jira
* Erfarenhet av Git eller andra versionshanteringssystem
* God kunskap inom Linux och containerteknologi
* Erfarenhet av CI/CD och automatiserade pipelines (exempelvis ArgoCD, Argo Workflows, GitHub Actions)
* Erfarenhet av pipelines för build, test (unit, integration, E2E), säkerhetsscanning och deployment
* Erfarenhet av säkerhetsscanning av container images och tredjepartsbibliotek
* Mycket goda kunskaper i engelska



  





*Meriterande - det är positivt om du har erfarenhet av:* 



* Programmering i Python eller Golang
* Kubernetes eller containerorkestrering
* AI-utveckling och AI-drift (MLOps)
* Machine Learning eller Deep Learning
* DevSecOps och säkerhetsfokuserad utveckling
* LLM-teknologier såsom prompting, RAG eller agentbaserade AI-lösningar
* Certifieringar inom Scrum, AI eller molnplattformar (AWS, Azure, Google)
* NVIDIA Developer-certifiering



  





*Detta är ett uppdrag hos en kund inom försvarsindustrin, där du får arbeta med avancerad teknik i samhällsviktiga system tillsammans med erfarna specialister.* 




  





*Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd.* 




  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 På Eccera får du de verktyg du behöver för att nå dina mål. Vi erbjuder alla våra konsulter kvalificerade utbildningar och certifieringar inom den senaste tekniken. Vi sätter stort värde på att våra medarbetare hela tiden ska fortsätta att utvecklas. Därför erbjuder vi dig möjligheten att lägga upp en individuell utbildningsplan, där vi bekostar såväl studiematerial som själva certifieringarna. Under din utveckling kommer vi matcha din kompetens mot rätt uppdrag i takt med att du avancerar.

title: Verksamhetsutvecklare inom Kontroll i Göteborg
description:
Placeringsort Flera orterDiarienummer FK 2026/009033
   

  

 Logga in och sök jobbet Öppnas i nytt fönster
   

  

*Varje år fattar Försäkringskassan 21 miljoner beslut som påverkar människors liv och hela samhället. Vi betalar ut cirka 260 miljarder kronor varje år. Det är allas skattepengar vi har hand om. Det är ett stort ansvar. Därför sätter vi oss in i varje ärende, följer lagar och regler, informerar och vägleder, bryr oss och visar omtanke. Det är ett jobb för dig som vill vara med och ta ansvar. Välkommen till Försäkringskassan.*
 Vi söker nu verksamhetsutvecklare till arbetet med att säkerställa korrekta utbetalningar och motverka bidragsbrott. Du ingår i en grupp med omkring tio andra verksamhetsutvecklare, vars arbete präglas av tätt samarbete. Vi arbetar utifrån agila principer och arbetssätt. Utöver dina närmaste kollegor, och kontrollutredningens olika verksamheter, samarbetar du med alla avdelningar inom Försäkringskassan samt flera andra myndigheter.
   

  

**Verksamhetsutveckling, analys och samverkan**
 Som verksamhetsutvecklare ansvarar du för att driva och stödja kundorienterad verksamhetsutveckling som bidrar till förverkligandet av Försäkringskassans vision och verksamhetsidé. I arbetet ingår att självständigt analysera komplexa frågor och kommunicera dem på ett begripligt sätt till olika målgrupper. Tjänsten innebär att förvalta, utveckla och förbättra befintliga, samt vid behov ta fram nya verksamhetsprodukter såsom exempelvis styrande dokument och processer. Du ansvarar även för att uppmärksamma utvecklingsbehov och omsätta dessa till konkreta aktiviteter, samt deltar i kravställning av IT-system.
   

  

 Som verksamhetsutvecklare hos oss behöver du självständigt skapa och underhålla nätverk med nödvändiga kontakter, samt upprätthålla goda relationer inom avdelningen och på andra avdelningar. Du har förmåga att såväl delta i arbetsgrupper som leda uppdrag. Du deltar även i samverkan med andra myndigheter och aktörer på nationell nivå. Tjänsten innebär att du hanterar sekretessbelagda uppgifter vilket ställer höga krav på rätt hantering. Därför är det viktigt att du som ska arbeta hos oss har högt säkerhetsmedvetande.
   

  

 Det är ett jobb för dig som vill vara med och ta ansvar.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som
   

  

* har högskoleexamen eller erfarenheter och kvalifikationer som kan prövas likvärdiga
* har flerårig erfarenhet från arbete inom aktuellt verksamhetsområde och eller verksamhetsutveckling
* har mycket goda kunskaper inom förändrings- och utvecklingsprocesser
* har goda kunskaper i projektledning och olika samverkansformer
* agerar på eget initiativ och tar ansvar för att nå goda resultat
* har förmåga att skapa ett användbart nätverk
* kan förstå och analysera komplexa frågor
* kommer med innovativa idéer och lösningar som främjar utveckling
* samarbetar effektivt med andra för att nå gemensamma mål
* har god kommunikativ förmåga, i både tal och skrift.


 Det är meriterande om du
   

  

* har erfarenhet från arbete som rör felaktiga utbetalningar och bidragsbrott
* har erfarenhet av agilt arbetssätt
* har erfarenhet av att arbeta i en stor offentlig organisation
* har arbetat med digitalisering och automatisering
* har erfarenhet av processutveckling och FK- VERA
* har goda kunskaper om förvaltningsrätt samt bidragsbrottslagen.


 Vi lägger stor vikt vid din personliga matchning, utifrån dessa egenskaper.
   

  

**Övrigt**
 Ett antal tjänster, tillsvidare, heltid, med tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Provanställning kan bli aktuellt. I enlighet med Försäkringskassans riktlinje för säkerhet genomförs bakgrundskontroller i denna rekrytering. I den här rekryteringen kan arbetspsykologiska test användas som en del i urvalsarbetet. Området har placeringsort Stockholm, Göteborg eller Umeå.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om Försäkringskassan som arbetsgivare? Besök fk.se/jobbahososs
   

  

**Kontaktpersoner**
 Chef: Charlotta Bunner Källberg, 010-116 94 52 (för frågor om tjänsten). HR-specialist: Carin-Maria Dahlberg, 010- 113 10 47, carin-maria.dahlberg@forsakringskassan.se (för frågor om rekryteringsprocessen). Kontaktperson ST: Jesper Brandt, 010-116 22 12. Saco-S: Matilda Eneqvist 010–118 33 81. Seko: Johanna Grundström, 010-115 30 44.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 19 april 2026. Ladda gärna upp dina examensbevis, betyg och intyg i samband med din ansökan.
   

  

 Har du skyddade personuppgifter eller ingen tillgång till bank-id ringer du den HR-specialist som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.
   

  

 Vi ska i allt vi gör, agera med utgångspunkt i principen om alla människors lika värde, rättssäkerhet och med god service.

title: Senior Manager, Product Management - Cartonboard
description:
**Job Description**
*At Billerud we believe that when we have tomorrow in mind, we will do our very best today. It is thanks to our employees that we are a world-leading company within the field of renewable paper and packaging materials. Do you want to realize your true value and work for a sustainable tomorrow? Join us!*
**We are now looking for a Senior Manager, Product Management - Cartonboard**
 A unique opportunity for a strategic, forward-thinking commercial leader who wants to shape the future of sustainable premium packaging.
   

  

 In this role, you will shape and steer the long‑term direction, competitiveness and profitability of Billerud’s Cartonboard portfolio to drive volume growth. Our renewable, high‑strength and fully recyclable fiber‑based packaging material is designed for premium consumer applications requiring durability, printability and a low climate impact.
   

  

 You will lead the strategic development of the segment’s product strategy, pricing, innovation roadmap and market offering, building a resilient and future‑ready portfolio that strengthens our position in a highly competitive global market while maximizing the segment’s contribution to Billerud’s success.
   

  

**Your main tasks will be**
*Strategy & Portfolio Leadership*
* Develop and steer the 3-year product management business plan for the Cartonboard segment to drive volume growth and profitability. This includes optimising the product group’s portfolio—covering product mix, innovation portfolio and product positioning.
* Drive value‑based market positioning and commercial argumentation for the product group, in close collaboration with the sales organization.
* Optimize the product portfolio and secure a profitable product mix aligned with S&OP/S&OE.


*Innovation & Market Development*
* Own and maintain the segment’s innovation plan (3–4-year horizon), continuously identifying new opportunities and ensuring a future‑fit portfolio.
* Contribute to cross-functional strategic initiatives driven by other parts of the organization.


*Pricing and Competitiveness*
* Provide recommendations for pricing strategy in close collaboration with the Senior Director Product Management and Business Area leadership.
* Support cost‑reduction initiatives together with Operations, helping strengthen competitiveness without compromising quality.


 You are part of our Business Area Board, and report to Senior Director Product Management. You will collaborate closely with Sales, Operations, Technical Service, Supply Chain and Business Development—creating strong alignment across the end-to-end value chain.
   

  

 This position is located at one of our locations: Solna Stockholm, Gruvön, Frövi, Skärblacka, Gävle or Karlsborg (Kalix). The role will require some travelling.
   

  

 At Billerud, we put Safety First. Together we take great pride in our responsibility to contribute to a safe work environment. Please read more about our Safety First initiative here.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 To succeed in this position, we believe you have:
   

  

* A university degree in a relevant field or working experience we find equivalent.
* 5+ years of experience in B2B commercial roles within Product Management, preferably within manufacturing or process industry, combining value‑based sales with solid technical product understanding.
* Proven experience in pricing strategy for global product portfolios and in driving business growth in cyclical markets.
* Experience managing an innovation portfolio and leading product development across multiple customer segments.
* Experience in change management and continuous improvement, with a strong results‑oriented mindset.
* Fluency in Swedish and English, with additional language skills considered an asset.


 Personal qualities
   

  

 For us, personality is important. To succeed in this position, we believe you are a strategic person who thrives in complex environments and easily translates market, customer and product insights into clear conclusions and direction. You are results‑oriented and driven by creating measurable impact on growth and profitability.
   

  

 Structured and organized, you plan your work effectively, set clear goals and ensure strong follow‑through. With strong communication and collaboration skills, you build alignment across stakeholders and internal functions. You influence and inspire others through clarity and engagement, driving decisions and change without formal authority. Curious, proactive and improvement‑minded, you challenge established ways of working and lead development initiatives that strengthen the business.
   

  

*Our values are very important for us and we believe that you recognize yourself in: We care for each other and We do what we say.*
 Contact
   

  

 If you want to know more about the role, you are welcome to contact recruiting manager Senior Director Product Management, Johan Wallin at Johan.Wallin@billerud.com
   

  

 If you have questions about the recruitment process, you are welcome to contact Group Senior Talent Acquisition Partner, Alice Lundberg at alice.lundberg@billerud.com
   

  

*We have chosen to manage this recruitment internally and therefore kindly ask external recruitment agencies to refrain from offering their services.*
 Application information
   

  

 Welcome with your application no later than
 **15 april 2026** 
 .
   

  

 Billerud is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates.
   

  

**Why us?**
* A key position to drive business growth for the Cartonboard Product Segment
* An opportunity to contribute to a more sustainable future
* Development and career opportunities
* Performance-based bonus, occupational pension and a number of different benefits according to company policy.
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard.


*Our recruitment process will contain: Interviews, recruitment test, references, alcohol- & drug tests and background checks.*
*Note: The formal position title for this role will be Product Management Senior Manager Cartonboard.*
 Click here to apply for the job!
   

  

 Do you want to help create a sustainable future through your work?
   

  

 At Billerud, we are fully committed to reduce our and our customers carbon footprint by offering high-performance paper and packaging materials. We are convinced that the right solutions can be important tools to help fight climate change. That's why we see it as our responsibility to enable the best packaging choices, for a better tomorrow.
   

  

 Our proud history in the forestry and paper industry goes back more than 150 years. Since then, we’ve grown to what we are today: a world leading company in high-performing paper and packaging materials - passionately committed to sustainability, quality, and customer value. Our materials are the basis of packaging and paper products used by millions of people around the world, 24 hours a day, all year round.
   

  

 We serve customers in more than 100 countries through our three business areas Liquid Packaging Board, Paper and Paper Board. Our nine production units are located in Sweden, USA, and Finland and we have around 6,000 employees in 19 countries. Our annual turnover is approximately SEK 43.5 billion and the share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. At Billerud we always put safety first, care for each other, and do what we say.
   

  

 Join us in redefining sustainable packaging and drive meaningful change for a better tomorrow.

title: Creative Lead Partner for In-house Agency at Group Brand Marketing & Communications
description:
**Job ID: 3252**
 Are you ready to shape the creative heartbeat of a leading financial brand? Are you a creative strategic thinker with a forward-leaning mindset of solving business challenges through innovative branded experiences? Keep reading.
   

  

 We are growing our inhouse agency team and now looking for a visionary creative to join a dynamic team that transforms impactful ideas through creative intelligence to make big measurable impact for the largest financial services providers in Nordics.
   

  

 We are for people who want to move forward
   

  

 When working at Nordea, you come as you are. We believe that great minds don’t think alike, and all perspectives are valuable to move forward for something better – for yourself, your colleagues, customers, shareholders and society at large.
   

  

 We are passionate about serving our customers, and we are dedicated to maintaining an inspiring and welcoming environment, daily, for our colleagues and partners. Because in the end, Nordea is all about making tomorrow a little better than today. That’s the Nordic Idea we’re built upon. We call it “The idea of something better”. You are welcome to join us on that journey.
   

  

**About This Opportunity**
 This position is based in any of the Nordic Capital cities within our in-house agency Nordea Group Creative.
   

  

 We are seeking a Creative Lead Partner to join our growing in-house agency team developing innovative, impactful 360 brand activations across the Nordics. We’re looking for someone who thrives at the intersection of creativity, strategy, and flawless execution - ready to shape bold ideas into unforgettable and authentic brand experiences.
   

  

 An entrepreneurial personality that is passionate about creativity and strategy, thrives in fast-paced environments, and loves being a true partner in the creative development process. If you love turning consumer insights into magic together with others to deliver world-class creative work, this is your moment.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
**Key Responsibilities**
* Lead through collaboration: Develop concepts for advertising and brand engagement to set the creative direction for brand activation ideas, customer value propositions and asset production projects aligned with business targets and brand platform identity.
* Partner for success: Work closely with creatives, project managers and other key stakeholders across the organization to translate complex challenges into compelling creative solutions and communication.
* Inspire teams and agency partners: Facilitate creative processes and guide copy writers, videographers and art directors to jointly achieve excellence in both development and execution.
* Champion innovation: Stay ahead of trends, technologies, media and cultural shifts to keep our creative output fresh and relevant for key target audiences in various channels and touchpoints.


**Who You Are**
* An experienced Creative Director and Strategist at a creative agency working with global brands.
* Strong portfolio showcasing creative excellence, strategic thinking and breakthrough ideas.
* Exceptional communication and collaboration skills.
* Champion a collaborative, solutions-oriented approach within cross-functional teams across markets.
* Ability to balance big-picture vision with hands-on execution.
* Passion for storytelling, design, and brand building.
* Collaborative mindset with a bias for action and problem-solving.


**Qualifications**
* Degree or Diploma in Advertising, Creative and Digital Development
* 7-10+ years of Marketing Communication Experience
* Proven ability to develop fully integrated omnichannel brand concepts from briefing to final production.
* Ability to research and uncover insights that connect brand, product, and consumer behavior.
* Fluent in English and at least one Scandinavian Language
