title: Virtualiseringsexpert
description:
Vill du jobba i en komplex miljö med en samhällsviktig funktion som ligger i teknikens framkant? Här finns stora möjligheter för dig som vill utvecklas både på djupet och bredden inom ett eller flera områden. Var med och gör samhället möjligt!

**Om jobbet**


Skatteverket går nu in i en ny fas av uppbyggnad och innovation då vi kommer att leverera it-tjänster och it-drift till fler statliga myndigheter. Vi skalar därför upp och stärker våra team med nya medarbetare som vill jobba med leverans av dessa tjänster till Sveriges myndigheter.


Skatteverkets kultur kännetecknas av ett agilt arbetssätt med högt i tak och en öppen dialog för förändring och utveckling. Du är en del av enheten Skatteverkets tekniska tjänster (STT) med cirka 500 medarbetare. Vi ansvarar för utveckling, underhåll och drift och levererar tjänster inom it-infrastruktur och plattformar till flera myndigheter. Du tillhör ett team där varje medlem har expertkunskap inom VMware.  

  

Som Virtualiseringsexpert är du en nyckelspelare på Skatteverkets it-avdelning. Du tillhör en sektion för server, virtualisering och lagring som utvecklar och underhåller Skatteverkets IaaS plattform. Just nu bygger vi nästa generation av plattformen, där vi ytterligare ska öka automatiseringen och vidareutveckla vår privata molnlösning för att hantera fler kunder. I dina arbetsuppgifter ingår utveckling och underhåll av Skatteverkets VMware infrastruktur tillsammans med teamet, du arbetar även med vår Aria Operations plattform.  

  

Vi erbjuder mycket goda semester-och tjänstepensionsvillkor samt förmåner för dig som är förälder. Vi är en stor IT-avdelning med cirka 1700 medarbetare, utvecklingsmöjligheterna är med andra ord många! I din anställning ingår flextid och det är en självklarhet för oss att du har balans mellan arbete och fritid.


Skatteverkets it-avdelning arbetar aktivt för att främja en inkluderande arbetsplats som präglas av jämställdhet och mångfald där alla medarbetare får lika möjligheter att bidra och utvecklas. Läs mer på Jobba med it hos oss ]

 **Om dig**


För att göra ett bra jobb och trivas hos oss på Skatteverket behöver du vara lyhörd, tänka nytt och ta ansvar. I arbetet är gott omdöme och förståelse för rättssäkerhet och säkerhet viktigt. Du ska också leva upp till vår medarbetarpolicy.


I det här jobbet ska du:

* ha god samarbetsförmåga, vara hjälpsam och ha ett gott bemötande
* förstå vikten av att skapa relationer med andra team för att nå de uppsatta målen
* ha förmågan och viljan att lära dig nya saker och kunna ställa om vid förändringar
* agera medvetet och professionellt utifrån en god självinsikt
* kunna analysera informationsflöden, se samband, mönster och göra avgränsningar
* uttrycka dig väl i tal och skrift på svenska.


Du ska även ha:

* flerårig aktuell och relevant arbetslivserfarenhet av VMware‑baserad on‑prem‑virtualisering i större miljöer
* erfarenhet av drift och förvaltning av affärskritisk infrastruktur
* erfarenhet av private cloud‑arkitektur och dess beroende


Det är önskvärt om du har arbetslivserfarenhet av Infrastructure as Code, Disaster Recovery strategi, block storage och lastbalanserare.

**Bra att veta**


Anställningen är tillsvidare och kan inledas med provanställning i sex månader.


En anställning hos oss kan innebära placering i säkerhetsklass. En säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) kan därför komma att genomföras före beslut om anställning. För en del av våra arbetsuppgifter i säkerhetsklass krävs svenskt medborgarskap.


På skatteverket.se/jobb kan du exempelvis läsa om vår samhällsbärande roll, fördelarna med att jobba hos oss och hur det går till när du söker våra jobb.


När du ansöker bifogar du ditt cv. Vi använder inte personligt brev i våra rekryteringar och du ska därför inte bifoga det i din ansökan.


Välkommen med din ansökan!


Skatteverket har en samhällsbärande roll. Vårt arbete berör alla privatpersoner och företag och genom vårt uppdrag bidrar vi till ett väl fungerande samhälle. Vår utveckling är kunddriven och vi samverkar med andra för att skapa effektiva lösningar. Tillsammans gör vi samhället möjligt.

title: P&C Business Partner Sweden
description:
Job Function: People & Culture The role:
* *Are you experienced in navigating change, acquisitions, and matrix environments, working across sales and consulting teams to deliver impactful People & Culture initiatives, while also handling day-to-day HR with a hands-on approach?*
* *Do you thrive in a small, close-knit team within a larger international organization, partnering strategically with leaders to shape decisions that strengthen performance, engagement, and culture?*
* *Are yo**u keen* *to join a global software and cloud leader, growing in a purpose-driven culture focused on innovation, collaboration, and impac**t?*

  

Location: **Stockholm, Sweden** | Work arrangement: **on-site**| Contract type: **Full-time**| Reporting to: **Regional P&C Leader / Lead P&C Business Partner** | Language requirements: **Fluent/professional Swedish & English, written and verbal**  

We are looking for a highly skilled **People & Culture Business Partner** to join our Nordic team. You will act as a trusted advisor to business leaders, supporting and advising on people related topics, driving initiatives that strengthen performance, engagement, and culture.
This position is ideal for someone with solid experience from international environments, preferably across both sales and consulting organizations, and with hands-on exposure to organizational change, acquisitions, and stakeholder management in complex matrix structures. **Key Responsibilities*** **Strategic P&C Partnership:** Partner with business leaders to understand goals, challenges, and workforce needs, ensuring P&C strategies align with business objectives, while acting as a trusted advisor in navigating organizational changes, integrations, and evolving business needs
* **Talent Management & Development**: Drive talent development initiatives, including performance management and career development, lead leadership and employee development sessions focused on skills, capability building, and continuous learning, and guide managers in goal setting, feedback processes, and performance evaluations
* **Employee Engagement**: Identify engagement opportunities within business units and co-create action plans with leadership, lead recognition, appreciation, and team-building initiatives, and analyze feedback from surveys and other data sources to provide actionable insights that improve engagement and retention
* **Organizational Development & Change Management**: Support leaders through change initiatives aligned with best practices in change management, strengthen team effectiveness, cultural alignment, and operational efficiency, and contribute to post-merger integration processes and organizational design work
* **Compensation & Rewards**: Provide strategic guidance on compensation decisions using market benchmarks and internal equity considerations. Support the design and optimization of compensation structures, commission models, and reward programs, and advise on promotions and salary adjustments
* **Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)**: Champion inclusive leadership and equitable people practices within business units, and support initiatives that strengthen diversity, belonging, and fair development opportunities
* **Data Analysis & P&C Reporting**: Use P&C data and metrics to identify trends and provide recommendations to business leaders, and prepare and present insights related to turnover, engagement, performance, and overall organizational health

  


What we need to see from you:
* Extensive proven experience as a People/HR Business Partner within an international organization
* Solid understanding of employment law and P&C best practices
* Hands-on understanding of sales organizations, particularly commission and incentive structures
* Competence within labor law and direct interaction with trade unions (e.g., consultations, negotiations, or compliance processes) as well as understanding of Collective Bargaining Agreements
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence leaders and build trusted relationships
* Experience supporting or working within a consulting/professional services environment
* Strong analytical skills and confidence working with HRIS and data tools

 **What we** **offer**
Our company benefits vary based on your location, but always include:* Global company culture: We defined our 6 core values through our company culture (https://www.softwareone.com/en/our-story/our-values).
* A mentor who supports your successful start and is at your side with advice and action.
* President’s Club: Recognition awarded by leadership and your peers.
* Lifelong education: A variety of training and development opportunities.
* Hire to Grow: We optimize our internal structures and feedback culture constantly to be your Employer of Choice, today and in future.

  


Why SoftwareOne?:  

SoftwareOne and Crayon have come together to form a global, AI-powered software and cloud solutions provider with a bold vision for the future. With a footprint in over 70 countries and a diverse team of 13,000+ professionals, we offer unparalleled opportunities for talent to grow, make an impact, and shape the future of technology. At the heart of our business is our people. We empower our teams to work across borders, innovate fearlessly, and continuously develop their skills through world-class learning and development programs. Whether you're passionate about cloud, software, data, AI, or building meaningful client relationships, you’ll find a place to thrive here. Join us and be part of a purpose-driven culture where your ideas matter, your growth is supported, and your career can go global.
Accommodations:

SoftwareOne welcomes applicants from all backgrounds and abilities to apply. If you require reasonable adjustments at any point during the recruitment process, email us at reasonable.accommodations@softwareone.com.
Please include the role for which you are applying and your country location. Someone from our organization that is not part of the decision-making process will be in touch to discuss your specific needs and we will make every effort to accommodate you. Any information shared will be stored securely and treated in the strictest of confidence in line with GDPR.  

At SoftwareOne, we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and teammates without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. Additionally, we encourage experienced individuals that have taken an intentional career break and are now prepared to return to work to explore our SOAR program.

title: Pre Sales Solutions Architect
description:
**Role – Pre Sales Solution Architect**


*Title:* Pre Sales Solution Architect


*Salary:* Negotiable / DOE


*Role Location:* Hybrid, office and client visit


*Language requirements:* Fluency in Swedish and English

 **About Us**

  

Integrity360 is the largest independent cyber security provider in Europe, with a growing international presence spanning the UK, Ireland, mainland Europe, Africa and the Caribbean. With over 700 employees, across 12 locations, and six Security Operations Centre's (SOCs)—including locations in Dublin, Sofia, Stockholm, Madrid, Naples and Cape Town—we support more than 2,500 clients across a wide range of industries.

  

Over 80% of our team are technical experts, focused on helping clients proactively identify, protect, detect and respond to threats in an ever-evolving cyber landscape. Our security-first approach positions cyber resilience as a business enabler, empowering organizations to operate with confidence.

  

At Integrity360, people come first. We invest heavily in learning, development and progression, fostering a dynamic culture where innovation, collaboration and continuous growth are at the heart of what we do. If you're ready to take your cyber security career to the next level, we’d love to hear from you.

 **Job Role / Responsibilities**

  

Pre-Sales Solution Architects work with Account Management and engage directly with customers up to and including C-level throughout to improve their cyber security maturity, first understanding their challenges and business issues, then working to develop the right technical and commercial solution.



You will be responsible for evangelizing, positioning, and architecting security technologies and managed service from within your assigned region or technology focus.



You will manage the solution design and be responsible for technical responses to clients via Presentations, Proposals and RFPs, where required drawing on subject matter expertise from across the business.



Your contribution to evolution of services is also key, working closely with Product Management on technology evaluations, contributing to the development of new managed services offerings, keeping Product Management informed about customer views and perceptions, related information security issues and implications for our solutions.



Most importantly, we are looking for a team player, someone who will fit in to the existing comradery. We work hard but have fun together in doing so.



Due to the nature of the role, there will be a need from time to time to travel within the country, and occasionally overseas.

 **Primary Duties/Responsibilities include:**


* Consulting with clients to understand their current security and business challenges in order to define a clear set of requirements and priorities.
* Propose innovative solutions and services that address these requirements; enabling customers to improve their security capability, level of security maturity and prioritise security investment to best mitigate customer’s risks, strengthen defences and reduce vulnerabilities.
* Build appropriate bills of materials, high level designs and scope up appropriate professional services engagements.
* Write compelling security and service-based customer-facing proposals, work with Bid Teams on RFI/RFP responses and work with account managers to develop associated commercials.
* Presenting proposed solutions to the customer in order to articulate the benefits and value of the solution and/or service.
* Architect blended solutions (Product, Professional and Management Services, including support options) that meet the business requirements of new and existing clients. Solutions should leverage components of I360 core products and fulfil all internal operational and support guidelines in line with our product/services catalogue.
* Leveraging key relationships with vendors to ensure solutions can be tailored from all available product/service providers.
* Experience in communicating with internal / external stakeholders with the ability to lead client meetings from a technical design perspective to drive an appropriate design. The ability to influence throughout the sales life cycle and enable delivery of a compliant solution.
* Clear communication with Professional Services & Managed Services Teams to ensure smooth delivery of projects.
* Experience in a role as a security consultant, providing high level technical consultancy in the design and implementation of multi-vendor networks.
* Successful track record and experience in bidding on complex cyber security tenders.
* Confirm Integrity360 bid meets the customers’ requirements and assist sales in technical qualification during the qualifying process
* Building up a trusted advisory relationship/partnership with our customers and providing them with advice and guidance on their security challenges.
Ability to negotiate with, and influence clients.  
* 

  

  

**Desired Skills**



Experience in the following technology, managed services and frameworks:


* Managed Service Experience such as Managed Detection and Response Services
* Penetration Testing Services and Tools
* NGFW – ideally Checkpoint or Fortinet
* SSE
* SOAR
* WAF
* Endpoint Security Solutions
* SIEM – Ideally knowledge of either Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel or Rapid7
* Vulnerability Management
* Cloud Security
* Familiar with Security Frameworks - Cyber Essentials, NIST, CIS Critical Security Controls
* Good understanding of cyber security threats, threat actors and TTP’s i.e., MITRE ATT&CK
* Fluency with both English and French to accommodate our multilingual clients

title: Senior Test Engineer – Backend & AI
description:
Vi söker en erfaren Test Engineer med fokus på backend-testning till ett engagerat och tvärfunktionellt team som arbetar med att utveckla en AI-driven tjänst. I uppdraget ingår ett nära samarbete med datavetenskap, backend-utvecklare och produktspecialister för att säkerställa kvalitet, tillförlitlighet och robusthet i hela lösningen.
  
Arbetsuppgifter
  
Du kommer att vara involverad i hela produktlivscykeln. Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden inkluderar:
  
Backend- och API-testning Designa, implementera och underhålla manuella och automatiserade testsviter för backend- och plattformstjänster. Genomföra funktionella, regressions-, integrations- och prestandatester. Reproducera, diagnostisera och dokumentera defekter med tydlig spårbarhet. Samarbeta för att förbättra teststrategi, testtäckning och arkitekturell kvalitet.
  
Data- och AI-testning Validera datainmatning, ETL-pipelines och datatransformationer i batch- och strömningsarkitekturer. Säkerställa korrekthet och integritet hos sensor- och tidsseriedata. Testa AI/ML-inferenspipelines, inklusive feature-generering, modellintegrering och outputkorrekthet. Bidra till automatiserade valideringssteg i MLOps-pipelines.
  
CI/CD, infrastruktur och verktyg Bygga och underhålla CI/CD-pipelines med integrerad automatiserad testkörning. Arbeta med Docker, AWS-tjänster och molnbaserade miljöer för att förbereda testmiljöer. Säkerställa testbarhet för hela kedjan: moln, backend och databearbetning.
  
Arbetssätt Arbeta i autonoma agila team med gemensamt ägarskap och hög tillit. Delta i backlog-refinement för att säkerställa tydliga acceptanskriterier. Kontinuerligt förbättra processer, standarder och teamets arbetssätt.
  
Krav
  
Erfarenhet av backendorienterad testning inom komplexa eller industriella system
  
Förståelse för meddelandeköteknologier såsom MQTT
  
Praktisk erfarenhet av Karate eller liknande ramverk för API/automationstestning
  
Erfarenhet av funktionell testning av mjukvara, webbapplikationer och webbtjänster
  
Kunskap om cybersäkerhetsrisker kopplade till digital produktsäkerhet
  
Stark drivkraft och förmåga att leverera i autonoma team med hög tillit
  
Kommunikativ, samarbetsinriktad och ansvarstagande
  
Nyfiken och med stark vilja att lära sig domänen och produkterna
  
Minst 3–5 års erfarenhet av mjukvarutestning
  
Flytande engelska i tal och skrift
  
  

Teknisk stack – obligatorisk
  
**Operativsystem:** Linux
  
**Skript och programmering:** Python, Bash
  
**Moln:** AWS
  
**Containerisering:** Docker, Docker Compose
  
**Versionshantering:** Git (GitLab, GitHub)
  
**CI/CD:** Erfarenhet av att integrera automatiserade tester i CI/CD-pipelines
  
**MLOps:** Förståelse för ML-livscykeln och testning av AI/dataorienterade applikationer
  
  

Meriterande
  
Förståelse för dataserialiseringsformat såsom Protocol Buffers (protobuf)
  
Erfarenhet av mobiltestning (iOS/Android)
  
Windows OS
  
Java eller andra programmeringsspråk
  
Azure Cloud
  
Kubernetes / EKS
  
GitHub Actions
  
  

**Start:** 2026-04
  
**Längd:** 6 månader (möjlighet till förlängning)
  
**Plats:** Göteborg (på plats, 100%)
  
Om Rasulson Consulting Rasulson Consulting är ett specialiserat bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag inom IT-sektorn. Vi samarbetar med både etablerade teknikbolag och innovativa startupföretag för att erbjuda attraktiva karriärmöjligheter för dig som vill utvecklas inom digitalisering och IT. Genom vår djupa tekniska kompetens och vårt omfattande nätverk matchar vi rätt konsult med rätt uppdrag – snabbt och träffsäkert. Hos oss får du personlig rådgivning, kontinuerlig återkoppling och möjlighet att ta nästa steg i din karriär.

title: Data Center Operations Manager
description:
Overview
  
In alignment with Microsoft values, we are committed to fostering an inclusive work environment where everyone can thrive. We are seeking a Datacenter Operations Manager (DCOM) to play a critical role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies that power Microsoft’s cloud services, including Azure, Office 365, Bing, Xbox, and OneDrive.
  
Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) organization operates a global portfolio of more than 100 datacenters and over one million servers, supporting more than one billion customers and 20 million businesses worldwide. CO+I is focused on operational excellence, sustainability, and continuous growth, while investing deeply in employee development through training, certifications, and career programs.
  
Join CO+I to help empower billions of people around the world.
  
Responsibilities
  
People Leadership
  
Lead through empowerment, accountability, coaching, and care.
  
Model Microsoft values and leadership principles.
  
Set clear team objectives, enable cross‑team collaboration, and support adaptability and learning.
  
Attract, retain, and develop talent by understanding individual strengths and career aspirations.
  
  

Datacenter Operations
  
Drive alignment across dependencies, supplier contracts, success metrics, resource readiness, and operational supportability.
  
Review and improve SLAs, KPIs, and OKRs across IT and Critical Environment (CE) services.
  
Partner cross‑functionally on global and regional initiatives, including EHS, risk assessments, and certifications.
  
Manage datacenter budgets, forecast demand, and make trade‑off decisions aligned to sound fiscal practices.
  
Ensure datacenter availability, safety, and security through strong execution and adherence to EHS runbooks.
  
Report and escalate safety or security issues immediately.
  
  

Service Delivery
  
Own end‑to‑end service quality, compliance, and customer satisfaction.
  
Manage client relationships and ensure expectations are clearly defined and met.
  
Apply subject matter expertise to meet SLA/OLA commitments with minimal disruption.
  
Share recurring issues and best practices with regional and global teams to prevent recurrence.
  
Plan staffing and resource needs based on current and future demand.
  
Ensure completion of required training and partner with Learning & Development to evolve training programs.
  
Participate in Sev 1 and Sev 2 incident bridges and approve RCA and post‑incident reviews.
  
  

Work Environment & Culture
  
Remove barriers to agility and enable teams to shift priorities without losing productivity.
  
Foster a culture of safety, quality, inclusion, and customer obsession.
  
Encourage collaboration across organizational boundaries and enable others to scale impact.
  
Delegate effectively to support growth and develop future leaders.
  
Act as a trusted advisor across a broad network of stakeholders.
  
Recognize and reward teamwork and contributions toward strategic goals.
  
  

Ownership
  
Demonstrate accountability for service quality, risk management, and customer experience.
  
Identify, resolve, or escalate unaddressed issues to ensure operational excellence.
  
Communicate risks, dependencies, and budget implications to support informed business decisions.
  
  

Qualifications
  
Required Qualifications
  
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience AND 5+ years of mission‑critical service management experience (e.g., IT services, manufacturing, logistics, military, or physical operations in IT or critical environments).
  
3+ years of experience leading diverse, technical teams.
  
  

Security Screening
  
Ability to meet Microsoft, customer, and/or government security screening requirements.
  
Must pass the Microsoft Cloud Background Check upon hire and every two years thereafter.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
6+ years managing large‑scale, complex projects or programs.
  
6+ years’ experience with Critical Environment infrastructure (e.g., UPS, generators, AHU).
  
6+ years’ experience with physical IT infrastructure (e.g., servers, networking, racks, cabling).
  
Experience managing budgets of $1M+.
  
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Supply Chain, or related field.
  
5+ years leading global or virtual technical teams.
  
Relevant certifications (e.g., ITIL, PMP, CDCP, CompTIA, Microsoft, networking or leadership certifications).
  
  

This position will be open for a minimum of five days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until filled.
  
Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer and provides reasonable accommodations during the application process.

title: Marketing Manager B2C
description:
Northmill Bank is a challenger bank at the intersection of technology and finance, committed to revolutionizing the way people manage and protect their financial well-being. We are creating a different kind of banking experience, digital yet personal.
  
Northmill Bank was founded in 2006 and have grown to over 200 employees in 3 countries, 4 000 merchants and 600 000 end users. We use the latest technology to develop safe, smart, and user-friendly products for our customers. They are the sole reason why we do what we do. We are a 100% cloud-based product company where technology is the driver to create smarter banking products.
  
Grab this opportunity to be a part of us and our journey!
  
About the role
  
As a Marketing Manager B2C, you play a key role in driving the growth, relevance and customer value of our consumer products in the market. You will be responsible for the entire process, from planning to execution of our B2C initiatives and ensuring that all campaigns are aligned with Northmill Bank's overall business objectives. In this role, you will report directly to our Chief Marketing Officer (CMO).
  
Responsibilities
  
  

You drive initiatives for Northmill Bank's B2C products all the way from idea to execution and follow-up.
  
  

You are responsible for developing and implementing marketing plans with a focus on customer acquisition, cross-selling, retention and reduced customer churn.
  
  

You collaborate cross functionally with the product and sales team to ensure successful product launches and unified go-to-market strategies.
  
  

You manage and optimize specific campaign and media budgets to ensure maximum ROI.
  
  

You monitor and analyze campaign metrics and use customer insights to optimize our strategies.
  
  

Who you are
  
To succeed and thrive in this role, you have a deep understanding of the entire customer journey, from acquisition and activation to loyalty. You are constantly up-to-date on new trends in B2C marketing, changes in consumer behavior and new marketing channels. You are used to managing both internal and external stakeholders, including media partners.
  
Qualifications
  
  

Bachelor's degree in marketing, economics, technology/engineering or equivalent.
  
  

More than 5 years of relevant experience in B2C marketing in a fast-paced environment, preferably in fintech or e-commerce.
  
  

Fluent in Swedish and English at a professional level.
  
  

Documented experience in successfully running and scaling consumer-facing marketing campaigns across multiple channels (digital, social media, CRM, etc.).
  
  

Strong experience in cross-functional collaboration with product and sales teams to drive holistic growth.
  
  

What we offer
  
  

A fantastic office in a prime Stockholm location with great spaces and views
  
  

An independent role with the opportunity to make a real impact
  
  

Great opportunities for professional development
  
  

Health - 5 000 kr health care allowance
  
  

Conference abroad every other year
  
  

Breakfast and fruits every day, as well as "holy fika” each Friday
  
  

Regular after work and celebrated successes at the office
  
  

Apply today and be a part of Northmill!

title: Kommunikatör med pressansvar
description:
Uddevalla – en plats för hela livet Välkommen att bli en del av vår växande kommun - och att tillsammans med oss arbeta för att ge våra kommuninvånare ännu bättre service. Vi erbjuder en attraktiv arbetsplats som ger dig stora möjligheter att utvecklas i din yrkesroll. Vårt mål är att alla ska kunna trivas och växa - under ett helt yrkesliv. Vi vill också utvecklas som arbetsgivare och arbetar aktivt för att på bästa sätt ta till vara på din kunskap och ditt engagemang. Vi uppskattar och uppmuntrar till medskapande från alla. Uddevalla kommun är en vacker plats vid havet i hjärtat av Bohuslän. Vi tror på ett öppet och positivt samspel mellan kommun, utbildning, näringsliv och kultur. Vi hoppas att du kommer att trivas hos oss!

**Behovet av bra och tydlig kommunikation ökar hela tiden och nu behöver vi förstärka vår kommunikationsavdelning med ytterligare en stjärna. Vi söker därför en erfaren kommunikatör och skribent med vana att hantera press och media.**

**Vad erbjuder vi?**
====================


Framför allt erbjuder vi ett roligt, meningsfullt och omväxlande arbete med positiva, stöttande och härliga kollegor! Du arbetar tillsammans med dina kollegor med kommunens externa och interna kommunikation men med ett extra ansvar att stötta organisationen när det gäller kontakten med press och media. Arbetet syftar till att öka genomslaget för kommunens kärnuppdrag och stärka förtroendet för kommunen som organisation och samhällsaktör.

**Om jobbet**
=============


Tillsammans med dina kollegor på avdelningen kommer du vara en del av det dagliga arbetet med kommunens externa och interna kommunikation, i alla våra kanaler. Ni arbetar med att identifiera, paketera och presentera information och nyheter och stärka bilden av Uddevalla som en attraktiv och framtidsinriktad kommun.


Det kan till exempel innebära att skriva nyhets- och informationstexter, fotografera, göra film, uppdatera våra hemsidor, arbeta med event och kampanjer samt kommunikationsplanering och -rådgivning, internt och externt. Vi hjälps åt för att skapa så bra intern och extern kommunikation som möjligt för våra medborgare och kollegor!


Vi har många fantastiska och spännande projekt och satsningar på gång inom kommunens alla verksamheter och vi hoppas att du vill vara en del av arbetet med att informera våra medborgare, medarbetare, företag, besökare och media om allt bra vi gör!

**Arbetsuppgifter**
===================

* Ansvara för kommunens mediekontakter, hantera inkommande medieförfrågningar och vara ett stöd till organisationen i mediefrågor.
* Skriva pressmeddelanden, skriva och svara på debattartiklar, krönikor med mera
* Följa medieläget och bedriva omvärldsbevakning och medieanalys som underlag för kommunikation, prioriteringar och ledningsstöd
* Bidra till kunskapsuppbyggnad inom organisationen för att stärka kommunens motståndskraft mot desinformation, ryktesspridning och försök till påverkan.
* Skapa innehåll till våra kommunikationskanaler, till exempel textförfattande, bild- och videoredigering.
* Strategiskt och operativt arbeta med kommunens kommunikation.

**Vi tror att du är**
=====================

* Van att hantera press och media
* Är trygg i en rådgivande roll
* Är en duktig skribent och klarar av att skriva olika typer av text
* Är noggrann, självgående, kreativ och idérik
* Är van att arbeta både strategiskt och operativt med kommunikation
* Är en lagspelare som trivs med att arbeta tillsammans med andra och inte har några problem att hjälpa till där det behövs
* En erfaren skribent med kunskap av strategisk och operativ kommunikation
* Har ett omvärldsintresse och en naturlig nyfikenhet på politik, samhälle och stadsutveckling


Stor vikt läggs vid personliga egenskaper.

**Kvalifikationer**
===================

* Högskoleutbildning inom kommunikation, marknadsföring, Public relations (PR), journalistik eller liknande utbildning/erfarenhet som arbetsgivaren bedömer likvärdigt
* Kunskap och erfarenhet av att arbeta inom kommunikation och marknadsföring
* Erfarenhet av att hantera press och media samt stötta ledningen i press-relaterade frågor
* Förmåga att anpassa innehåll efter målgrupp och plattform
* Kunskap om lagar och regler kopplade till digital kommunikation, till exempel Dataskyddsförodningen (GDPR), Lagen om tillgänglighet till digital offentlig service och Upphovsrätt
* Kunskap och erfarenhet av att arbeta med program inom Adobe Creative Cloud och Office-paketet
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i en politiskt styrd organisation


Anställning sker under förutsättning att erforderliga beslut fattas. För tjänster där arbetet innebär direkt och regelbunden kontakt med barn och ungdomar, samt för verksamhet inom LSS, vård- och omsorg, hälso-och sjukvård, psykiatrisk vård och missbruksvård, ska giltigt utdrag ur polisens belastningsregister visas upp alternativt lämnas in före anställning. Tänk på att det kan ta tid att få utdraget så beställ det i god tid för att rekryteringsprocessen inte ska fördröjas. När du söker ett jobb hos oss sparas din ansökan. Det beror på att vi är en offentlig verksamhet. Din ansökan blir en allmän handling när vi har mottagit den. Det innebär att ansökningshandlingar och information om vem som sökt en tjänst kan begäras ut av den som vill. Om du har skyddad identitet ska du inte göra din ansökan digitalt utan kontakta den som står som kontaktperson i annonsen. För dig som inte är medborgare inom EU/EES-området krävs arbetstillstånd, detta gäller inte dig som har permanent uppehållstillstånd. Arbetstillståndet ska uppvisas vid intervjutillfället. I länken nedan finns samlad information kring de viktigaste förmånerna som finns i kollektivavtalet som vi har i Uddevalla kommun. Informationen handlar om bland annat semester, lön och ersättningar, föräldraledighet, försäkringar, kompetensutveckling och pension.Säg hej till ditt kollektivavtal 2024 Vi undanber oss vänligen men bestämt direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt försäljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

title: Technical Solutions Engineer / Integration Solu...
description:
We're thrilled that you're considering a career at Kindly! Here's everything you need to know about this job.
  

  

You will join the Customer Integrations team as we evolve it into a **Professional Services function**. The team is technically strong but early in its journey around client advisory, structured problem solving, and consultative delivery. Note that this is a technical, non-commercial role.


We are looking for a **Technical Solutions Engineer / Integration Solutions Engineer** who can lead by example, challenge existing approaches, and help raise the bar technically and professionally.

 **What you will do:**

* Work directly with customers to translate business needs into technical solutions, including shaping scope and tradeoffs.
* Design and build scalable, production-grade integrations between our AI agent platform and third-party systems.
* Act as a technical advisor - confidently challenging assumptions and proposing better approaches.
* Own integrations end-to-end: architecture, implementation, testing, release, and reliability.
* Partner with Sales and CSM teams to scope integrations and identify expansion opportunities.
* Help define repeatable integration patterns, tooling, and documentation as we build our Professional Services model.
* Raise standards internally by improving architecture, communication, and engineering best practices.

 **Who we think you are:**

* 3-5 years of experience as a Software Engineer, Solutions Engineer, or in a customer-facing engineering role.
* Experience running technical discovery sessions, scoping integrations, or translating messy business requirements into clean technical specs.
* Strong hands-on experience with Python, TypeScript, JavaScript and NodeJS. If you can do all four, you get extra points.
* Hands-on experience with REST APIs, webhooks, and cloud platforms; reading and navigating third-party API documentation comes naturally to you.
* Able to extract unclear requirements from non-technical stakeholders and turn them into something buildable
* Strong system design instincts and experience owning code in production.
* Clear communicator with good business awareness - you understand how technical decisions drive customer value.
* Confident enough to push back, but pragmatic and collaborative in how you do it.
* Structured way of working with the ability to meet deadlines.

 **It’s a bonus if you have experience with:**

* Experience in solutions engineering, technical consulting, or IT management consulting.
* GCP, Firebase, Kubernetes.
* CI/CD, automation, and developer productivity tooling.
* Postgres, Valkey, or similar databases.
* Formal CS or engineering background.
* QA experience.

**What success looks like****:**

* Integrations are more reliable, scalable, and easier to repeat.
* Customers see you as a trusted technical partner, not just an implementer.
* The team adopts more structured, consultative ways of working.
* Customer integrations start to feel like a capability, not bespoke one-offs.

**Why join:**


This is a chance to shape how we work with customers while staying deeply technical. You’ll have influence without a heavy hierarchy, and the opportunity to grow with the team as Professional Services matures.

 **About Kindly:**


Kindly is a growing SaaS company of 70+ employees, working out of Oslo, Stockholm, Copenhagen, and Amsterdam. Our AI agent platform helps big businesses and organizations around the world to automate and deliver world-class customer service. We leverage AI, GPT, and machine learning to build the world's most loved chatbot. We have over 150 incredible clients that include Booking.com, Kahoot!, Voi, Norwegian, and Happy Socks – just to name a few. Our tech reaches over one million users every month, and we handle thousands of messages every hour.

Professional Services
Locations
Stockholm

title: Enterprise Security Architect
description:
**Introduction**
As Autoliv continues its digital transformation, the role of Enterprise Security Architect is critical to ensuring that security is embedded not just in our systems—but in our strategy. In this role, you will define the long-term direction of cybersecurity architecture across the organization, working closely with Enterprise Architecture to align security capabilities with business priorities, risk appetite, and future needs. This is a strategic leadership position for someone who sees the big picture, understands the details, and can connect the two to drive meaningful, secure change.**Mission**
To define, align, and govern Autoliv’s long-term cybersecurity architecture across business units, technologies, and global initiatives. The Enterprise Security Architect sets the direction for secure transformation—partnering closely with the Enterprise Architecture function to embed security into strategic planning, target states, and solution blueprints across the entire organization.
This is a future-looking, cross-domain role that ensures our security architecture evolves with the business, supports innovation, and upholds trust at scale.**Key Responsibilities****Strategic Architecture Leadership**
Define and maintain Autoliv's enterprise security target architecture, aligning with business strategy, digital transformation, and regulatory frameworks.Translate enterprise risk and business objectives into architecture principles and actionable guidance for all security domains.
**Cross-Domain Integration**
Work across domains (IT, OT, Product, Cloud, AI, Data, Supply Chain) to ensure security-by-design is embedded consistently in all architecture layers.Collaborate with Enterprise Architecture, Infrastructure, Digital Engineering, and Business Solution Architects to co-develop secure, scalable patterns.
**Governance & Standards**
Lead the development of security architecture frameworks, design principles, and reference models across domains.Drive adoption of standardized controls, reusable patterns, and security capability models (e.g., aligned with NIST CSF, SCF, TOGAF).
**Transformation Enablement**
Provide architecture direction to major digital transformation initiatives, including AI/ML orchestration, manufacturing modernization (CSiM), and cloud platform strategy.Review and guide solution designs for strategic programs to ensure enterprise alignment and security assurance.
**Risk & Business Alignment**
Ensure security architecture supports risk-informed decision making, regulatory compliance, and performance visibility.Connect technical design to business impact by linking architecture outcomes with resilience, agility, and trust.
**Communication & Leadership**
Act as a visible security thought leader in architectural forums and governance boards.Educate and influence senior stakeholders on the value of security architecture in achieving long-term business goals.
**Key Competencies & Skills**
10+ years in security architecture roles, including experience with global enterprise environments.Deep understanding of architecture frameworks (TOGAF, SABSA), risk management (ISO 27005, FAIR), and security standards (NIST CSF, SCF, ISO/IEC 27001).Proven experience translating business strategy into actionable architecture.Ability to lead cross-functional collaboration and facilitate enterprise-level decision-making.Broad knowledge of security domains: IAM, cloud, data protection, OT, AI, DevSecOps, supply chain security.
**Why This Role Matters**
Our transformation journey spans everything from connected factories to intelligent edge, AI, and global services. As Enterprise Security Architect, you will ensure that this journey is secure, aligned, and resilient—helping Autoliv lead with confidence and integrity in a changing world.
**Department**
Information Technology (IT)
**Locations**
Autoliv Sweden, Autoliv Romania
**Remote status**
Hybrid
  
Autoliv Sweden
Autoliv Romania
Outstanding People Embracing Change
Our position as a worldwide leader in automotive safety systems, saving 37,000 lives every year, is achieved by our approximately 65,000 outstanding colleagues around the world. Their diverse assets, breadth and depth of expertise and desire to develop, in combination with Autoliv’s ambition to enable fulfilling careers, supports our vision of Saving More Lives.
Learn more about our culture and Key Behaviours

About Autoliv Group
Autoliv is the worldwide leader in automotive safety systems. Through our group companies, we develop, manufacture and market protective systems, such as airbags, seatbelts, and steering wheels for all major automotive manufacturers in the world as well as mobility safety solutions.
At Autoliv, we challenge and redefine the standards of mobility safety to sustainably deliver leading solutions. In 2025, our products saved 40,000 lives and reduced 600,000 injuries.
Our ~64,000 colleagues in 25 countries are passionate about our vision of Saving More Lives and quality is at the heart of everything we do. We drive innovation, research, and development at our 13 technical centers, with their 17 test tracks.

title: Erfaren teknisk projektledare till EDF Projekt inom molnteknik
description:
*Vill du påverka och göra skillnad på riktigt? Här bidrar du till att skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen. Det du gör spelar roll.*


Din roll


Vill du vara med och bidra till att skydda människor och samhället? Gillar du att samarbeta mellan europeiska motparter och vill öka framträdandet inom försvarsteknik? Vi söker en erfaren teknisk projektledare att ta ansvar för ett spännande och strategiskt projekt finansierat via European Defence Fund (EDF) – EU:s centrala instrument för att utveckla nästa generations försvarsteknik och säkerhetsförmågor. Denna roll kommer att fokusera på European Defence Operational Collaborative Cloud (EDOCC), en gemensam europeisk molnplattform som syftar till att stärka interoperabilitet, datasäkerhet och operativa insatser mellan medlemsländer.


I rollen kommer du bland annat att:

* Leda storskaligt, långsiktigt utvecklingsprojekt med internationella partners och EU-finansiering.
* Bygga och stärka ett nära samarbete med europeiska intressenter både inom industri och försvar.
* Leda och koordinera Saabs del i EDOCC alla faser enligt EDF:s regelverk och rapporteringskrav.
* Säkerställa leveranser enligt tidplan, budget och kvalitetskrav.
* Ansvara för kommunikation med projektpartners, finansiärer och interna intressenter.
* Identifiera och hantera risker, förändringar och beroenden i en komplex projektmiljö.
* Säkerställa efterlevnad av säkerhetskrav och regulatoriska ramverk.
* Driva styrgruppsarbete och rapportering till ledning och finansiär.
* Ta fram nödvändiga dokument och rapporter.


Det här är en unik möjlighet att kombinera ledarskap, avancerad teknik och geopolitiskt viktiga initiativ genom att forma Europas gemensamma moln- och säkerhetsarkitektur.


Tjänsten är placerad i Solna, i nyrenoverade, moderna kontorslokaler mitt i en av Stockholms mest attraktiva kommuner. Lokalerna är nyinredda med fokus på arbetsmiljö, ljus, ergonomi och modern teknik. När du kombinerar de nyrenoverade lokaler i Solna med internationella resor får du en roll som både binder ihop Europa och utgår från en modern, fräsch svensk bas.


Resor förekommer i tjänsten.


Din profil


Vi söker dig som är tydlig och strukturerad i ditt arbetssätt, med en god kommunikativ förmåga. Du har lätt för att lösa problem, även under pressade situationer, och trivs i en roll där du får stötta och leda andra. Du är resultatorienterad men samtidigt flexibel nog att anpassa dig efter förändrade förutsättningar. Med en visionär blick kombinerar du strategiskt tänkande med en praktisk inställning, och du värdesätter samarbete som en nyckel till framgång.


Det här behöver du ha med dig:

* Minst 8–10 års erfarenhet från en liknande projektledarroll.
* Flytande kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både muntligt och skriftligt, då vi använder dessa språk i vårt dagliga arbete.
* Erfarenhet av att ha drivit eller deltagit i projekt inom European Defence Fund (EDF) ses som starkt meriterande.


Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.

  

Vad du blir en del av


Upptäck oändliga möjligheter. Anta utmaningar, skapa smarta innovationer och uppgradera dina förmågor. Det här är en plats för kluriga tänkare, modiga pionjärer och alla däremellan. Tillsammans uppnår vi det extraordinära, var och en tar med sitt unika perspektiv. Varje bidrag räknas.


Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 26,100 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.


Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här


Vänligen observera att vi arbetar med löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum har gått ut.
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: Senior Manager, Product Management
description:
**Senior Manager, Product Management - Pagero**

Pagero is part of Thomson Reuters. Together, we are building the world’s largest, open business network. With our cloud-based network, customers can reach any business, anywhere in the world – no matter how many borders their operations may cross. We take care of the technical and regulatory requirements across the entire order-to-cash, purchase-to-pay and freight processes, to give customers data accuracy and security, transparency, and real-time visibility. All this through a single connection.


We are looking for a Senior Manager to join our Team.


The Product Senior Manager – ONESOURCE Pagero manages end to end the CTC/eInvoicing Compliance Pagero product to achieve the growth goals of ONESOURCE Pagero. This role coordinates Product Managers with special focus on Core and CT/Compliance services within Pagero product.


**About the Role**

In this role as Senior Manager, Product Management you will:


* Take ownership of strategy, design, business requirements, feature and functionality tradeoffs and release schedule for ONESOURCE Pagero CTC/eInvoicing offering.
* Partner with sales / product marketing to develop marketing strategy and product launch activities
* Direct and influence cross-functional teams in order to ensure dependencies for proposition, product or service delivery are met
* Be responsible for gathering and prioritizing client feedback and understanding client needs in order to drive innovation and increase client adoption and retention
* Be responsible for developing comprehensive product definitions, concept proposals and business cases which result in marketable products and services.
* Be involved in budgeting activities, development of 3rd party partnerships, etc.
* May support commercial facing teams in client engagement and retention activities
* Manage a team of Product Managers in the areas of ONESOURCE Pagero Core and Compliance
* Be responsible for developing talent within the team
* Be responsible for reviewing current products to ensure that quality and currency are being maintained
* Participate in revenue planning and pricing decisions for new products
* Manage AI projects within the product team to boost efficiency and speed up new solution delivery.

**About You:**

You are fit for the role of a Senior Manager, Product Management, if you have the following required qualifications:


* Educational background in areas of system integrations and/or IT Project Management and/or similar tech-oriented educational background
* You are a driven, ambitious and independent person
* Excellent communication skills
* Experienced with or interested in managing software integration/implementation projects
* Experience in management and leadership to team leaders or product managers
* Experience on prioritisation and work allocation: assign work, allocate resources, monitor results, control costs and manage staff
* You can understand the full picture, as well as dive deep into the details
* Minimum 8 years work experience
* Fluent English skills (speaking and writing)
* Successfully pass an extended background check (criminal and credit)

**Additional preferred qualifications include:**

* Deep knowledge and understanding of ONESOURCE Pagero and processes, in particular our CTC and eInvoicing compliance offering.
* You are self-driven and have both technical, business and communication interest to improve Pagero Network and processes
* Understanding of our customers general business processes and needs connected to O2C and P2P compliance

#LI-AB3


**What’s in it For You?**

* **Hybrid Work Model:** We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
* **Flexibility & Work-Life Balance:** Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
* **Career Development and Growth:** By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
* **Industry Competitive Benefits:** We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
* **Culture:** Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
* **Social Impact:** Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
* **Making a Real-World Impact:** We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.
**About Us**

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.


We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.


As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.


We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here .


Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here .


More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com.

title: Customer Service Specialist with Swedish (W/M) - REMOTE
description:
We're firm believers that happiness is attainable for everyone. That's why we offer our customers the widest selection of home and garden furniture at attractive prices, operating in 19 European markets and constantly expanding our reach.

Our motto, **'Delivering Happiness,'** isn't just a slogan. It's the driving force behind everything we do. From the happiness of our customers to the fulfilment of our employees, this philosophy guides us in every aspect of our daily work. At Beliani, we cultivate a vibrant team spirit, where over 700 friendly and talented individuals tackle new challenges together every day.

If you thrive on continuous growth, embrace challenges, and seek a dynamic work environment, seize the opportunity to shape the future of e-commerce with us today!

**Your Main Tasks Will Be:**

* Telephone and e-mail service for Swedish-speaking individual clients
* Presenting Beliani products to our clients
* Chat service
* Managing the timely execution of orders and return shipments
* Handling customer service inquiries pre- and post-sale via phone, e-mail, and chat

REQUIREMENTS

* **Communicative level of English (min. B1 level)**
* Strong written and verbal communication skills
* Good cultural knowledge of a specific market

title: P&C Business Partner Sweden
description:
**Why SoftwareOne?**  

SoftwareOne and Crayon have come together to form a global, AI-powered software and cloud solutions provider with a bold vision for the future. With a footprint in over 70 countries and a diverse team of 13,000+ professionals, we offer unparalleled opportunities for talent to grow, make an impact, and shape the future of technology. At the heart of our business is our people. We empower our teams to work across borders, innovate fearlessly, and continuously develop their skills through world-class learning and development programs. Whether you're passionate about cloud, software, data, AI, or building meaningful client relationships, you’ll find a place to thrive here. Join us and be part of a purpose-driven culture where your ideas matter, your growth is supported, and your career can go global.  

  

**The role**  

* *Are you experienced in navigating change, acquisitions, and matrix environments, working across sales and consulting teams to deliver impactful People & Culture initiatives, while also handling day-to-day HR with a hands-on approach?*
* *Do you thrive in a small, close-knit team within a larger international organization, partnering strategically with leaders to shape decisions that strengthen performance, engagement, and culture?*
* *Are yo**u keen* *to join a global software and cloud leader, growing in a purpose-driven culture focused on innovation, collaboration, and impac**t?*

  

Location: **Stockholm, Sweden** | Work arrangement: **on-site**| Contract type: **Full-time**| Reporting to: **Regional P&C Leader / Lead P&C Business Partner** | Language requirements: **Fluent/professional Swedish & English, written and verbal**  

We are looking for a highly skilled **People & Culture Business Partner** to join our Nordic team. You will act as a trusted advisor to business leaders, supporting and advising on people related topics, driving initiatives that strengthen performance, engagement, and culture.
This position is ideal for someone with solid experience from international environments, preferably across both sales and consulting organizations, and with hands-on exposure to organizational change, acquisitions, and stakeholder management in complex matrix structures. **Key Responsibilities*** **Strategic P&C Partnership:** Partner with business leaders to understand goals, challenges, and workforce needs, ensuring P&C strategies align with business objectives, while acting as a trusted advisor in navigating organizational changes, integrations, and evolving business needs
* **Talent Management & Development**: Drive talent development initiatives, including performance management and career development, lead leadership and employee development sessions focused on skills, capability building, and continuous learning, and guide managers in goal setting, feedback processes, and performance evaluations
* **Employee Engagement**: Identify engagement opportunities within business units and co-create action plans with leadership, lead recognition, appreciation, and team-building initiatives, and analyze feedback from surveys and other data sources to provide actionable insights that improve engagement and retention
* **Organizational Development & Change Management**: Support leaders through change initiatives aligned with best practices in change management, strengthen team effectiveness, cultural alignment, and operational efficiency, and contribute to post-merger integration processes and organizational design work
* **Compensation & Rewards**: Provide strategic guidance on compensation decisions using market benchmarks and internal equity considerations. Support the design and optimization of compensation structures, commission models, and reward programs, and advise on promotions and salary adjustments
* **Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)**: Champion inclusive leadership and equitable people practices within business units, and support initiatives that strengthen diversity, belonging, and fair development opportunities
* **Data Analysis & P&C Reporting**: Use P&C data and metrics to identify trends and provide recommendations to business leaders, and prepare and present insights related to turnover, engagement, performance, and overall organizational health

  


  

**What we need to see from you**  

* Extensive proven experience as a People/HR Business Partner within an international organization
* Solid understanding of employment law and P&C best practices
* Hands-on understanding of sales organizations, particularly commission and incentive structures
* Competence within labor law and direct interaction with trade unions (e.g., consultations, negotiations, or compliance processes) as well as understanding of Collective Bargaining Agreements
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence leaders and build trusted relationships
* Experience supporting or working within a consulting/professional services environment
* Strong analytical skills and confidence working with HRIS and data tools

 **What we** **offer**
Our company benefits vary based on your location, but always include:* Global company culture: We defined our 6 core values through our company culture (https://www.softwareone.com/en/our-story/our-values).
* A mentor who supports your successful start and is at your side with advice and action.
* President’s Club: Recognition awarded by leadership and your peers.
* Lifelong education: A variety of training and development opportunities.
* Hire to Grow: We optimize our internal structures and feedback culture constantly to be your Employer of Choice, today and in future.

  


  

**Job Function**  

  

People & Culture
  

**Accommodations**  

SoftwareOne welcomes applicants from all backgrounds and abilities to apply. If you require reasonable adjustments at any point during the recruitment process, email us at reasonable.accommodations@softwareone.com.
Please include the role for which you are applying and your country location. Someone from our organization that is not part of the decision-making process will be in touch to discuss your specific needs and we will make every effort to accommodate you. Any information shared will be stored securely and treated in the strictest of confidence in line with GDPR.  

At SoftwareOne, we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and teammates without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. Additionally, we encourage experienced individuals that have taken an intentional career break and are now prepared to return to work to explore our SOAR program.

title: Finance Systems & Planning Controller
description:
Are you interested in finance, systems, and turning data into insights? We’re looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Controller to join our finance team. In this role, reporting to CFO, you’ll play a key part in strengthening our financial processes, ensuring accurate reporting, and supporting informed decision-making across the organization. If you enjoy working with financial systems, data analysis, and collaborating with different teams to drive improvements, this could be a great opportunity for you.

 **Role and Key Responsibilities**  

This role goes beyond traditional controlling, combining financial planning, systems ownership, and group-level reporting. You will play a key role in driving financial structure, insights, and process improvements across the organization while supporting strategic decision-making.

 **Key responsibilities include:**

* **Budgeting and Forecasting:** Lead the annual budgeting process and periodic forecasting for the group. Coordinate budgets and prepare forecasts for group functions.
* **Reporting:** Prepare reports and analyses for the audit committee and Board of Directors.
* **Financial Planning and Analysis:** Develop and maintain financial models to support strategic initiatives. Analyse financial performance and provide insights to management.
* **System Administration:** Act as system administrator for financial systems (ERP, Power BI, and planning tools).
* **Cost Control:** Ensure accurate cost reporting at group level and for common functions, including classification and consistency.
* **Business Partnering:** Collaborate with management and business units to provide financial guidance and support decision-making.
* **Process Improvement:** Continuously improve financial processes and systems to enhance efficiency and accuracy, including harmonizing processes, systems, and control activities across the group.

**Key Skills and Competence**

* Finance
* Analytical skills
* Communication and interpersonal skills
* Problem-Solving
* Attention to Detail

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, or related field (Master’s degree or professional certification such as CPA/CMA is a plus).
* Proven experience as a Controller or similar role with at least 5 years of experience, preferably from international and/or listed environment.
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Excellent knowledge of financial principles and practices.
* Experience from working in Excel, PowerPoint, consolidation systems (preferably IBM Controller and IBM Planning Analytics), ERP systems (preferably NetSuite) and BI tools such as Power BI.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Language requirements: English (Enea Corporate language).


To succeed in this role, you are goal-oriented and work in a structured and organized way. You have a strong analytical mindset and the ability to see connections from a broader perspective. You take ownership of your work and have a hands-on approach to getting things done. At the same time, you value collaboration and enjoy working with others to achieve common goals.

**What can Enea offer you?**  

At Enea, we strive to create an environment where you can thrive both professionally and personally. We offer:

* **Flexible work:** A modern work model that supports a healthy work–life balance.
* **Collaborative environment:** A dynamic, globally connected team culture that fosters engagement in cybersecurity initiatives and cross-regional projects.
* **Inclusive culture:** A diverse and inclusive workplace that values your voice and promotes mutual respect and collaboration.
* **Competitive compensation:** A competitive salary package complemented by region-specific benefits.
* **Ongoing learning and career development:** Continuous learning opportunities and clearly defined career paths to support your professional growth across the organization.

  

Does this sound exciting? We would love to learn more about you! Apply today – we review applications on an ongoing basis and may fill the position before the final deadline.

 **About Enea**:


Enea is a global specialist in advanced telecom and cybersecurity software, with a vision to make the world's communications safer and more efficient. Through continuous innovation, our solutions help customers optimize, secure, and monetize their global communication services, enabling seamless connections between people, businesses, and connected devices worldwide. We serve 300 customers across 100+ countries, including telecom operators, cloud communication providers, governments, and cybersecurity vendors. More than 3 billion people rely on Enea technologies every day. Headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, Enea is publicly listed on NASDAQ Stockholm. www.enea.com

title: Customer & Performance Director
description:
**EcoDataCenter** is one of Europe’s leading AI hubs, designing, building, and operating data centers in Sweden for the most demanding cloud and AI workloads. Our mission is to ensure that the digital infrastructure society depends on operates reliably, 24/7. We are the forefront of a new generation of data centers - setting the standard for sustainability, safety, and quality within our industry.


With us, you will work with state-of-the-art technology in a Swedish company with deep roots and a strong local presence in Dalarna. Here, traditional industrial craftsmanship meets cutting-edge technology — from power systems, cooling, and backup generation to sophisticated monitoring and control systems.


Our vision is to create a world where digital infrastructurepowers global innovation and local progress.

**About the Role:** As Customer & Performance Director, you ensure a professional and consistent customer experience throughout the entire delivery cycle. You take ownership of transparent, high‑quality service delivery in line with SLA requirements, lead the structured handling of service requests, and ensure efficient access and visit processes. You take a leading role in developing customer dialogue and driving quality, efficiency, and business value across our services. The role includes close collaboration with internal key functions such as the local Operations teams, Sales, Construction, and Technology.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Customer Communication & Relationship Management**

* Ensure proactive and clear customer communication for planned work, incidents, and service delivery matters.
* Build and maintain professional, trustworthy customer relationships.
* Manage tactical customer dialogues together with the Operations Manager.

**Service Request Management**

* Lead the structured and traceable handling of service requests, including Smart/Remote Hands.
* Provide fast, clear, and high-quality feedback to customers.

**Service Performance & SLA/OLA**

* Follow up on service performance through regular service reviews.
* Ensure correct SLA/OLA follow‑up and deliver standardized reports.

**Proactive Customer Management & Business Development**

* Identify and lead improvements in collaboration with customers and ensure that actions are completed as agreed.
* Identify opportunities for additional sales where it adds customer value.

**Your Profile:** You are a structured and communicative leader who brings clarity and stability to service delivery processes. You understand the dynamics of service delivery in critical operational environments and can translate SLA requirements into practical workflows, follow‑up routines, and meaningful customer interactions. Your working style reflects commitment, courage, and reliability — you take responsibility, build trust, and contribute to a culture focused on respect, performance, and continuous development.


Your background includes:

* Several years of experience in customer‑facing roles within advanced technical service delivery or complex operational environments.
* Strong experience in managing large global clients in high-demand environments, preferably including SLA/OLA follow-up and driving service performance.
* Experience leading teams or demonstrated leadership potential.
* Excellent communication skills and ability to work with structured reporting, formats, and standards.
* Strong ability to create structure and ensure consistent process follow‑up.

title: Risk & Compliance Director
description:
**EcoDataCenter** is one of Europe’s leading AI hubs, designing, building, and operating data centers in Sweden for the most demanding cloud and AI workloads. Our mission is to ensure that the digital infrastructure society depends on operates reliably — 24/7. **About the Role:** As the Director of EcoDataCenter’s Risk & Compliance function, you will lead and advance our company-wide efforts to ensure a secure, well‑governed, and fully compliant operation. You will shape and evolve our frameworks for risk management, HSE, information security, and IT governance, while driving a cohesive risk and compliance agenda across multiple sites and functions.

 **Key Responsibilities:** In this role, you will lead and advance the company’s overall approach to risk, security, and compliance - at both strategic and operational levels. You will lead the development and adoption of key frameworks and ensure that the organization is equipped, aligned, and resilient to meet future demands.


Your main responsibilities will include:

* Lead and evolve the company’s risk and compliance agenda, closely aligned with organizational goals and business strategy.
* Lead transformation and ensure that frameworks, policies, and regulatory requirements are successfully adopted and continuously followed.

* Build a strong risk-aware culture and enhance safety, accountability, and internal control practices throughout the organization.
* Develop and maintain a modern, structured approach for risk identification, classification, and barrier management.
* Oversee an effective nonconformity process, including CAPA, ensuring timely and sustainable resolution.
* Lead the planning and execution of internal and external audits, ensuring accurate and well‑structured evidence management.
* Define and maintain frameworks for metrics, performance indicators, and reporting
* Build, lead and empower your team, fostering growth and strengthening capabilities within risk and compliance.

**Your Profile:** To succeed in this role, we are looking for someone with experience leading and developing teams, as well as lead change and development work. You have the ability to combine clear requirements with a business-minded approach and an understanding of the organization’s operations. You have strong analytical and communication skills and experience reporting to the management team, as well as fostering risk awareness, structure, and continuous improvement.


Your background includes:

* Several years of experience in risk, compliance and quality within complex and regulated industries.
* Extensive experience in establishing and further developing governance frameworks, internal controls, and audit processes, including IT and information security governance from a governance and risk perspective.

title: Critical Infrastructure System Director
description:
**EcoDataCenter** is one of Europe’s leading AI hubs, designing, building, and operating data centers in Sweden for the most demanding cloud and AI workloads. Our mission is to ensure that the digital infrastructure society depends on operates reliably — 24/7. At the same time, we set the standard for sustainability, safety, and quality within our industry.


With us, you will work with state-of-the-art technology in a Swedish company with deep roots and a strong local presence in Dalarna. Here, traditional industrial craftsmanship meets cutting-edge technology — from power systems, cooling, and backup generation to sophisticated monitoring and control systems.


Our vision is to create a world where digital infrastructurepowers global innovation and local progress.


With a growing site in Falun, we are now taking the next major step: building a new megacampus in Borlänge. At the historic Kvarnsveden paper mill site, we are creating the future workplace for Dalarna — a hub for sustainable, high-tech data centers.

**About the Role:** As a Critical Infrastructure System Director, you lead and evolve NOC (Network Operations Center) and OT (Operational Technology) operations to deliver secure, stable, and scalable performance across multiple facilities. You take ownership of governance, architectural integrity, resilience, and continuous improvement across monitoring, OT platforms, and critical infrastructure systems. You will work closely with Datacenter Campus on local actions and feedback, with Operational Excellence on standards and improvement methodologies, and with Risk & Compliance on OT risks, access control, and audit evidence. Together with Customer & Performance, you will ensure clear communication on incident status and planned maintenance windows.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

* Ensure robust 24/7 monitoring, including triage, logging, clearly defined response times, and established escalation procedures.
* Lead the stable and secure management of OT platforms and CI systems (BMS/EPMS), overseeing integrations, backup and recovery processes, patch management, controlled access, and vendor coordination.
* Develop, test, and maintain business continuity and resilience plans for OT/NOC and CI systems, ensuring regular drills and clearly defined fallback procedures.
* Be responsible for establishing and maintaining a clear, consistently applied alarm policy, ensuring proper naming, tagging, reduction of nuisance alarms, and high data quality - including follow-up and measurable improvements.
* Ensure high‑quality documentation and evidence to support incident, problem, and change processes, as well as audit requirements.
* Be responsible for root cause analyses and corrective actions, and ensure that handover requirements, system documentation, and baselines meet the criteria for “Ready for Operation.”

 **Your Profile:** To succeed in this role, you bring a strong interest in technology and relevant education in the field. You have a solid understanding of systems in complex operational environments and proven experience leading teams.


Your background includes:

* Experience in critical operations and maintenance, preferably including 24/7 monitoring and complex technical systems.
* Solid knowledge of critical infrastructure, including electrical power, cooling, control systems, and OT/ICS environments.
* Solid knowledge of OT and control systems such as BMS/EPMS, monitoring and alarm systems, and system integrations.
* Experience managing and developing technical systems or platforms in production environments, with a strong focus on stability and security.
* Solid knowledge of system documentation, baselines, and structured incident, problem, and change management processes.
* Documented experience in root cause analysis and structured improvement initiatives, with the ability to turn insights into concrete actions.
* Fluency in Swedish and English, spoken and written.

 **Employment type:**  

Form of employment - Permanent employment  

Employment rate – 100 %  

Working hours - Daytime  

Location – Borlänge  

Collective agreement – Tech Alliance
